[[Troye Sivan]] (young; ''[[X-Men Origins: Wolverine]]'')}} {{Quote|Nature made me a freak. Man made me a weapon. And God made it last too long.|Logan|Logan (film)}} '''Logan''' (born '''James "Jimmy" Howlett'''), also referred as '''Wolverine''', was a [[mutant]] with retractable forearm claws, enhanced physical attributes, and accelerated regenerative abilities. As the co-leader of the X-Men, he was also a famous hero and warrior, having fought in many wars throughout human history. After leading the X-Men for many years and earning a legendary reputation, Logan became old and vulnerable due to the poisoning of his adamantium skeleton, resulting in his healing factor failing. While taking care of [[Professor X/Revised Timeline|Charles Xavier]] with the help of [[Caliban]], Logan found a daughter in [[Laura]], causing [[Zander Rice]] and his [[Donald Pierce|men]] to pursue them, until they defeated them both, including his own clone, [[X-24]], whom he was murdered by gruesomely. Accepting the fate that he always wanted, Wolverine died in peace from his wounds with Laura beside him. ==Biography== ===Early Life=== James "Jimmy" Howlett was born in 1832 in what would later be known as the Northwest Territories of Canada. In 1845, James lay sick in his bed while [[Victor Creed|Victor]], who was visiting him, kept him company as James' [[John Howlett|father]], John Howlett, arrived to check in on him. A few moments later, Victor's father, and John's groundskeeper, [[Thomas Logan]] (who was also having an affair with John Howlett's wife), entered the Howlett home, drunk, looking for James' mother, [[Elizabeth Howlett]], and had a confrontation with John, which resulted in Thomas shooting John. James then heard the gunfire and went to investigate, finding John dead. [[File:JamesHowlettYoung3-XMOW.png|left|thumb|250x250px|James' powers begin to manifest.]] It was under this grief-stricken incident that, in an act of vengeance, James' mutation began to manifest as he sprouted bone claws, slowly emerging from his fists and knuckles. In a wild rage, James roared in rage and fury, and, using his claws, impaled Thomas. However, before dying, Thomas revealed that John wasn't James' real father. Much to James' horror, Thomas revealed with his dying breath that he was James' true father by telling him he is also his son. After Thomas died, Elizabeth, horrified by James' mutation, cast her son out by calling him a freak as she questioned James as to what he was. Confused about what was happening to him, James fled and ran into the woods with Victor following behind him. After running away, Victor, who was also a mutant, caught up and overtook James. Victor said that they were brothers and they both vowed to stick together and look after each other no matter what, which eventually led them to a life of rage and violence. As Victor was truly his own family since they shared a father, James started going by the name 'Logan'. [[File:LoganCreedCivilWar-XMOW.png|thumb|250x250px|James and Victor fight in wars over the next 128 years.]] Over the next 128 years, as the two boys grew into men, Logan and Victor fought bravely together as soldiers for the United States in numerous wars. The wars the two brothers participated in throughout their years together included the American Civil War (both participating on the Union side led by American President Abraham Lincoln), World War I (on the Western Front), World War II (both participating in the D-Day Invasion), and the Vietnam War using their mutant powers. However, Victor grew increasingly violent and uncontrolled as time passed.{{Cite film|X-Men Origins: Wolverine}} ===Meeting Ichirō Yashida=== [[File:Logan02-TW.png|left|thumb|250x250px|Logan saves the life of Ichirō Yashida from the Nagasaki bombing.]] Sometime in 1945, Logan transferred from the European front to the Pacific front, where he is captured by Japanese forces prior to August 9th and sent to a POW camp near [[Nagasaki]]. He was put into a form of solitary confinement in a nearby well, topped with a tank turret. Logan was a witness to the {{W|Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki|Nagasaki bombing}} on August 9, 1945, where he saved the life of [[Ichirō Yashida]], a young officer in the {{W|Imperial Japanese Army}}. As the force of the nuclear blast rolled toward them, Logan grabbed Yashida before he could commit [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Seppuku seppuku] with his comrades and forced him into the well, covering him with an iron plate, shielding Yashida from the fire of the blast, the officer suffering a small burn on his face that would leave a permanent scar. Logan himself, horrifically burned, survived and healed, much to Yashida's astonishment. They remained underground for an indeterminate time until Logan felt it was safe for them to surface. Yashida thanked Logan and offered him a Japanese {{w|katana|samurai sword}} as a token of gratitude. Logan refused, said to hold onto it for him, and promised to return for it in the future. ===Meeting Charles and Erik=== [[File:Wolverine-XMFC.png|thumb|250px|Logan in 1962]] In 1962, while traveling the world searching for mutants to fight the [[Hellfire Club]], a young [[Charles Xavier]] and [[Erik Lehnsherr]] came across Logan in a bar. They introduced themselves, but before they could continue, he harshly dismissed them. ===Affair With Gwen=== In January 1973, Wolverine was hired as a a [[Gwen|young woman]]'s bodyguard to but started a sexual relationship with her against the rules. He was discovered the next morning. ===Becoming Weapon X=== {{Quote|If men were meant to fly we'd grow wings.|Wolverine|X-Men Origins: Wolverine}} During the Vietnam War in the 1970s — the last war Logan and Victor participated in — Victor attacked and attempted to rape and kill a young Vietnamese village woman. When soldiers from his platoon and commanding officer tried to stop him, Victor killed the commanding officer, then brutally attacked the soldiers who tried to stop him. Logan came to his aid and reluctantly assisted Victor, released his claws, and prepared to defend his brother. Logan was ready to fight for him as promised, revealing their gifts to the other men in their unit. For their grave insubordination, this incident led to Victor and Logan to be sentenced to death and placed in front of a firing squad to be executed after decapitating the senior officer. They were shot by a firing squad but did not die, as their regenerative healing factors kept them alive. [[File:LoganCreedVietnam3-XMOW.png|left|thumb|250px|Logan and Victor are sent to be executed by a firing squad.]] They survived the execution and death sentence and, because of their unique regenerative healing abilities, both mutants were placed in a holding cell soon after. Logan and Victor were eventually brought to the attention of Major [[William Stryker]], who visited and confronted them in the cell. Stryker asked them to join his special team of mutants called [[Team X]] as a way out of imprisonment. Victor and Logan accepted and joined other mutants - [[Deadpool|Wade Wilson]], [[Agent Zero]], [[Chris Bradley]], [[Blob|Fred Dukes]] and [[John Wraith]] - on covert missions throughout the world. During one of their missions, while under Stryker's command, Team X was sent to search for and retrieve a mysterious meteorite - later discovered to be [[adamantium]] - from a diamond trafficking operation in [[Lagos]], [[Nigeria]]. They attacked a compound there and after defeating the compound's security forces, obtained a mysterious rock from the compound's leader and discovered that it originated from a remote village. Upon arriving at the village, Stryker and the team them interrogated nearby villagers to see if there were any other meteorites and were informed that the rock fell from the sky as a meteor. Stryker wanted more information and had Wade Wilson interrogate in his place, being the only one who could speak in the local tongue. When they threatened the village as to where the meteorite came from, the village chief refused to tell Stryker the origins of the meteorites, as they believe them to be sacred. After receiving no further answers, Stryker ordered his team to attack the villagers, first directing Victor to kill the chief. The whole village descended into chaos as Stryker's team started killing the civilians. Logan stopped the mutants when they started attacking a nearby village. [[File:LoganCreedNigeria-XMOW.png|thumb|250px|Logan quits Team X after Victor begins to kill innocent people.]] After a brief argument with Victor, Logan quit the team and said he couldn't kill innocent people, being disgusted by the murders committed by his teammates, Stryker's willingness to kill innocent civilians, and Victor's acceptance of this. He promptly left and abandoned Victor and the group before walking into the night, ignoring Victor's calls to him. With his departure, Team X disbanded. Eventually, only Agent Zero and Victor stayed faithful to Stryker. Six years after leaving Team X, Logan was living in western Canada with a school teacher named [[Kayla Silverfox]], residing in a cabin high in the Canadian Rockies, and working as a lumberjack. After many years of warfare, Logan desired a more quiet and tranquil life. Kayla manipulated him into a state of complacency with her power, comforting him from the nightmares of the past. One day, Agent Zero and Stryker visited Logan at his job to inform him that Bradley and Wade had been recently killed. Before doing so, Stryker asked Logan if he remembered Agent Zero, with Logan responding by asking Zero if he was still shooting first and asking questions later. Zero responded by pulling out his concealed gun, shooting Logan's cigar, and, after putting away his gun, asked Logan a follow-up question as to if he's still chewing on cheap cigars. Stryker told Logan he has a job for him and Logan responded by telling Stryker he already had a job. Zero humorously questioned Logan's job payment as a lumberjack by asking if it pays 18 grand a year. Logan corrected him, saying his pay was actually 18.5 grand a year, before adding that he hadn't had to kill anybody lately. Zero asked if Logan was starting to miss it, and Logan, irritated, replied that he was now before Stryker broke them up with an order to Zero to get back into the car. One night, Kayla told Logan about a spirit who was tricked into being parted forever from his lover, the Moon. Stemming from her Native American ancestry, she recited a story to Logan about a spirit named Kuekuatsheu who fell in love with the moon but was fooled by the Trickster into stepping foot in the mortal world from which he could never return. Thus, the spirit, Kuekuatsheu, was parted forever from the moon, and was forced to look at the Moon forever but never be with her again, howling at her whenever he saw her. One day, while Logan was at work, Kayla was attacked by Victor. Sensing Victor's presence in the area, he rushed to Kayla's abandoned car but found that he was too late for any kind of rescue. Kayla was dead and, from the claw marks left on the abandoned car, Logan realized this was Victor's doing. [[File:Wolverine 1920x1080.jpg|thumb|250px|Logan confronts Victor over the murder of Kayla.]] That evening, he found Victor in a bar and, in a state of rage, lunged at him. After a brutal fight, Victor snapped off Logan's bone claws on his left hand before leaving. The next morning, Logan woke up in an emergency room, where Stryker happened to be present. Stryker told Logan that he could help him exact revenge on Victor with an experimental procedure involving adamantium. Logan agreed to go through with it, but warned that he is solely bent on revenge. [[File:43 (3).jpg|left|thumb|250px|Logan escapes the Weapon X facility.]] Prior to the adamantium procedure, Logan asked for new dog tags with the name "Wolverine" on them - a reference to the story Kayla told him. During the excruciatingly painful procedure, Logan's skeleton was laced with molten adamantium. His heartbeat flatlined, leaving Stryker and his team believing their subject had died. After a few moments, though, he resuscitated and escaped the facility after overhearing Stryker order that his memory be erased for so that his DNA could be used for an upcoming project. Logan continued to flee until he found and hid in a barn. The elderly couple who owned the barn offered him food and shelter. Although wary of him at first, they soon warmed up to him, even giving Logan a jacket which belonged to their son and allowing him to take their motorcycle for a spin. However, after offering Logan breakfast, Agent Zero killed the couple. The barn was bombed as Logan escaped on the bike. After a long and daring chase, he attacked Zero's helicopter and took it down. [[File:Blob vs Wolverine.jpg|thumb|250px|Wolverine fights Blob in a boxing match.]] After killing Zero, Logan attempted to locate the two remaining members of Team X and found [[John Wraith]] and [[Fred Dukes]] in [[Las Vegas]]. Intent on killing Victor in revenge for Kayla's death and Stryker for his betrayal, Logan tried to inquire Dukes about Stryker's activities. A misunderstanding ensued, leading to a boxing match between the two. Logan ended up beating Dukes and interrogated him until Dukes told him about a mutant and former prisoner named Remy LeBeau, or as most people called him, [[Gambit]]. Logan and Wraith found Gambit in [[New Orleans]] and asked him about [[The Island|Stryker's Island]], where mutants were being held hostage for Stryker's new project, [[Weapon XI|Weapon XI/Deadpool]]. [[File:4564.jpg|left|thumb|250px|Wolverine fights Gambit after believing he was going to take him back to the Island.]] Gambit, who believed that Logan wanted to take him back to the island, attacked him. Logan then saw Wraith's dead body with Victor crouched next to it, collecting samples of Wraith's DNA. Enraged, after knocking out Gambit, Logan engaged in a fight with Victor. In the battle, Logan gained the upper hand and was preparing to kill Victor. However, Gambit, who had regained consciousness during the battle, attacked Logan from a rooftop, allowing Victor to escape. After defeating Gambit, Logan clarified his intentions and convinced Gambit to take him to the island. With Gambit's help, Logan arrived at the Island, which turned out to be [[Three Mile Island]]. After Logan arrived on the Island, he found Stryker, who then told him about Deadpool and reveals that Kayla (who was present on the island) wasn't really killed. She was revealed to be alive and reluctantly working for Stryker. As Victor was watching through the window from a nearby room, Wolverine is told that Victor and Stryker were also working together to capture various mutants, which led to Wolverine being tricked into agreeing to have adamantium bonded to his skeleton by using his rage against Victor from killing the woman Logan loved. Kayla had, at some point, been recruited by Stryker for the purpose of seducing Logan and creating an incident to motivate him to join the Weapon X program and participate in the adamantium procedure - her faked death. After Logan discovered that Kayla was not only alive but also working for William Stryker, he likened her to the Trickster who played Logan as the fool. This hurt Logan deeply and, feeling betrayed after learning this, he decided to leave the facility and move on with his life. [[File:WolverineVictorFight.jpg|thumb|250px|Wolverine fights Sabretooth at Three Mile Island.]] Wolverine, having heard Kayla's screams and calls of distress, rushed back to her rescue, engaging in a fight with Victor yet again. Wolverine attacked Victor and they battled each other, with Logan ultimately gaining the upper-hand and after defeating him in combat, Wolverine managed to pin his half-brother down and was in a position to kill Victor. Logan, finally getting the chance to decapitate and kill Victor, spared him when Kayla intervened and talked Logan out of it. She convinced him that he would be no better than Stryker, saying that Logan was not an animal while Victor taunted him. Logan, listening to Kayla's pleas, chose not to give into his animal instincts, and instead punched and knocked Victor out, sparing him. Following the fight between Victor and Logan, Kayla said that she only did what Stryker wanted her to do because her sister, [[Emma Frost (Origins)|Emma Frost]], was being held a captive on the island along with other mutants, some of whose powers were pooled into the Deadpool project. Kayla then told Logan that she agreed to take part in Stryker's plans because he had promised to release her sister if she did so. Kayla assured Logan that her feelings for him were genuine and that their love was real. He agreed to help her and they made their way to free the mutant prisoners [[File:Xmen-wolverine-movie-screencaps.com-10278.jpg|left|thumb|250px|Wolverine rescues the mutant prisoners including Emma and [[Psylocke|Elizabeth Braddock]].]] After freeing the imprisoned mutants, while leading them out of the facility, Logan and the mutants were stopped and confronted by Weapon XI, controlled by Stryker. Logan ordered the others to find another way out so that he could engage this new abomination on his own without putting the other mutants in danger. They clashed as the other mutants fled. Using Chris Bradley's power, Stryker was able to control Weapon XI to kill Wolverine. With the other powers he had gained from several other mutants (including Cyclops' optic beams, Wolverine's healing power, John Wraith's teleportation, Chris Bradley's technopathy, and Wilson's prized blades used as two retractable claws), he was ready to fight and kill Wolverine. Logan climbed one of the nuclear reactor's cooling towers with Weapon XI in pursuit. Weapon XI followed in an instant using Wraith's teleportation ability. Logan put forth his best effort but was pinned down. Just as Weapon XI was about to decapitate him at the command of Stryker, Victor shoved Weapon XI away, which made him fall into the reactor and took Logan with him. Victor caught Logan and told him that the only reason he saved him was so he could kill him himself, saying, "Nobody kills you but me." Logan and Victor eventually tag team and ended up back to back with Deadpool teleporting in front of each of them back and forth. They eventually caught and stabbed him; however, Deadpool managed to teleport to the other side of the reactor. Deadpool's built-in optic blasts activated, threatening both Logan and Victor. As Deadpool fired his optic blasts, Victor attacked Deadpool as he fired on Logan with his optic blasts. Logan fell over the edge of the reactor as Victor was shot back while jumping at Weapon XI. Logan climbed back onto the reactor just as Weapon XI was blasting Victor into the ground. Just before Deadpool could kill Victor, Logan ran, jumped, and slashed Weapon XI through the neck, successfully decapitating him. Weapon XI held his neck as Logan kicked him into the nuclear reactor cooling tower, his head falling off as he tumbled below, with the lasers of his optic blasts still firing, blasting into and destroying the tower. [[File:LoganVsDeadpool2-XMOW.png|thumb|250px|Wolverine cuts off Weapon XI's head.]] After defeating Deadpool, Logan helped Victor out of the hole and coldly informed Victor that, despite his help, their relationship was over, telling him that they're done. Nothing had changed between them, as Logan stated that he wanted nothing more to do with him. Victor responded, saying that they could never be finished and reminded Logan that they were still brothers, and that brothers should always look out for each other. Victor then jumped off the side of the reactor cooling tower. As the reactor started to tumble, Logan followed, collapsing as he landed. Just before a piece of the reactor landed on Logan, Gambit saved him by breaking up the piece. Gambit and Logan had then briefly split up, with Gambit going to help the children and Logan attending to Kayla. Logan carried her a few steps before Stryker shot him in the back. In a feral rage, he turned and sprinted at Stryker, eventually jumping after him. Mid-leap, Logan was shot in the head by Stryker with adamantium bullets and fell to the ground, unconscious, damaging his brain and losing much of his long-term memory. Just as Stryker was about to kill Kayla, she grabbed his ankle and, using her power, ordered him to leave, before dying. Logan awoke, and unbeknownst to him, he had forgotten everything and everyone, including Gambit and Kayla. However, when he saw Kayla's corpse, he shut her eyes, laying her to rest. Gambit and Logan soon went their separate ways. In an added scene at the end of the movie, Wolverine was seen drinking at a bar in Japan. When the bartender asked if he was drinking to forget, Wolverine replied that he was actually drinking to remember. ===Joining the X-Men=== {{Quote|Hey, bub, I'm not finished with you yet.|Wolverine|X-Men (film)}} Years later, Logan was seen as a cigar-smoking loner who wandered from place-to-place in Canada. He participated in cage fights to make money, taking advantage of the enhanced durability that his adamantium skeleton and healing factor granted him. [[File:XMENSTILL-016.jpg|left|thumb|250px|Wolverine in a cage match.]] [[Rogue|Marie]], going by the name Rogue, walked inside a building where a crowd was cheering at a caged wrestling match beside a bar, which she watched. A match just ended, and it seemed that it was not the winner's first victory that night. A new wrestler entered the cage and a new battle began. At first, the new person seemed to have the upper hand, but the winner recovered at an amazing speed and fought back, easily defeating the new person in mere seconds. The winner and still champion was announced – Wolverine. Marie watched him in awe. Later that night, after the fights are over, she was sat down at the bar quietly. Wolverine sat near her, ordering a beer. He looked at her, as she seemed out of place in the bar. In the background, a news report was telling about the upcoming gathering of world leaders in New York and that they would be talking about many topics, including the mutant phenomenon. The wrestler from earlier approached Wolverine and demanded his money, saying that no man could take a beating like that without a mark, implying that Logan must be a mutant. Wolverine would not budge, and the wrestler pretended to give up, but then pulled a knife and went to attack. Marie screamed at Wolverine to look out, and he jumped out of his chair and unsheathed his razor-sharp metal claws on the man's neck. [[File:XMENSTILL-007.jpg|thumb|250px|Wolverine holds his claws after being attacked for being a mutant.]] The barman threatened him with a shotgun. With superhuman speed, Wolverine slashed at the shotgun, slicing easily through it. Wolverine then left the bar and Marie ran after him, realizing he was a mutant. Thinking that he would understand, she got into his [[Logan's Pickup Truck|truck]] and hid in the trailer as he drove away. Later, on the road, Wolverine noticed something in the back. He stopped the truck, got out, and checked the cart, where he soon found Marie, hiding. She apologized and says that she needed a ride. After he discovered Marie, Wolverine kicked her out by ordering her out into the cold, then began to get back into the truck. She yelled that she saved his life, but he said that she did not and started driving away. After a few meters, guilt made him stop and Marie entered the truck before he kept driving, thus allowing her to accompany him. During the drive, they began to converse and slowly formed a father/daughter relationship. After he gave her something to eat, she introduced herself as Rogue. She asked him questions about his life, but he did not answer. He noticed that she was cold, turned on the heater, and told her to put her hands on it. While doing so, he almost touched her hands but she pulled them away. At first, he took it as fear, but she explained that when people come into physical contact with her skin they get hurt. She looked at his hands and asked him if it hurt when his claws come out. He answered that it hurt every time, and then asked her what kind of a name Rogue was. She asked the same question about his name, Wolverine, and he told her that his name was Logan. She told him that her name was Marie and then advised him to wear his seat belt. He told her that he didn't need driving tips just before they slammed into a tree branch and Logan was thrown through the windshield a few dozen meters away into the snow. The truck started burning as Rogue awoke and saw Wolverine get up. He walked back and asked her if she was alright, to which she answered that she was stuck after watching in shock how his wounds healed. He walked over to help her, but his hyperactive senses picked on the scent of a foe – Sabretooth. [[File:XMenWolverine-MAIN2.jpeg|left|thumb|250px|Wolverine in the snow after the car accident caused by Sabretooth.]] Suddenly, Sabretooth jumped out of the woods and threw Wolverine. He then threw him again onto the hood of the truck and walked over to him, towards Rogue, who was already terrified from the fire in the truck that had started and was creeping towards a gas canister. Then, two figures showed up behind Sabretooth – [[Cyclops]] and [[Storm]]. Cyclops put his hand to his visor and shot a powerful optic blast at Sabretooth. He then used his beams to release Rogue, and they grabbed her and Wolverine and ran away seconds before the truck exploded. Cyclops and Storm took the rescued pair to the [[X-Mansion]]. Wolverine was lying in the X-Men's base, unconscious. [[Jean Grey]] was taking care of him. She tried giving him an injection but he woke up, thinking her an enemy. He managed to escape her grasp easily and ran away through the tunnels of Xavier's mansion. He was not sure which way to go, but then started hearing voices in his head – [[Charles Xavier]], telepathically guiding him. He found a closet with clothes and took a shirt before an elevator opened right next to him. He entered and it automatically closed, taking him to the upper floors. He hid from some mutant kids running around and kept running, straight into a classroom, where Xavier was teaching a class. Xavier greeted him hello and then finished the lesson. The students left, and then one of them, a girl named [[Shadowcat|Kitty]], ran back because she had forgotten her purse. She then exited by phasing herself through the door. Logan looked confused and the professor introduced himself as Charles Xavier, and offered Logan breakfast. Logan asked where he was, and Xavier answered that he was in Westchester, New York – he was attacked and his people had brought him there for medical attention. Logan said that he didn't need any medical attention and asked where Rogue is. Xavier answered that she was there too and that she was fine. Logan's rescuers entered. Xavier introduced them as Ororo Munroe, AKA Storm, and Scott Summers, AKA Cyclops. Scott held out his hand to shake with Logan, but Logan did not respond. Xavier told him that they saved his life and Jean entered. Xavier introduced her as well, and then told Logan that he was in Xavier's school for Gifted Youngsters – mutants, and that he was safe there from Magneto. Logan asked, "What’s a Magneto?", and Xavier answered that Magneto was a very powerful mutant and that the mutant who attacked him was an associate of Magneto's called Sabretooth. Logan thought the whole thing was ridiculous and started to leave, roughly telling Scott to get out of his way. Xavier then told him that he could help him figure out who he was. Telepathically, Xavier reminded him that it had been 15 years since Logan lost his memory, displaying his telepathic skills that could help Logan remember his past. Intrigued, Logan asked about what the place was. Xavier told Logan more about the school and convinced him that it was the best place for Rogue to be in. He then told him that the school was only a cover and that the lowers levels were completely different. Xavier showed Logan the hangar containing the X-Men's supersonic jet, the [[X-Jet|Blackbird]]. He explained that when he was a boy he discovered he could control people's minds and that when he was seventeen, he had met a young man named Erik Lensherr, who had the ability to create and control magnetic fields. Believing that humanity would never accept them, Erik had grown angry at humanity and became Magneto. Xavier offered Logan a deal – 48 hours to discover why Magneto was after him, in exchange to help uncover his forgotten past. In the X-Mansion, Jean was running tests on Logan. He apologized for hurting her earlier and tried to flirt with her, to which Jean responded with a sharp look and promptly put him into the scanning machine. Later at night, Logan had seemingly accepted Xavier's offer to help him. Jean showed Logan to his room, where he would be staying for the night. Logan tried to resume flirting with Jean. He was jealous of her relationship with Scott and jokingly asked her if her gift was to "put up with that guy." Jean answered that she was telekinetic - she could move things with her mind. Logan asked, "What kind of things?" and she closed the closet door to demonstrate. She then said that she also had some telepathic abilities, though hers weren't nearly as powerful as the professor's. Logan told Jean to read his mind but she initially refused. After a moment of silence, though, she agreed and put her hands to his head. She saw images of fire and the operation he went through, and stopped. Logan eagerly held her hands and asked her with great anticipation to tell him what she saw, but Scott entered, interrupting them. Jean left slightly embarrassed, and Scott and Logan taunted each other about Jean and about how Scott had to rescue Logan. Before he left, Scott told Logan to stay away from his girl. [[File:Hugh-xmenpromo010.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Wolverine stabs Rogue by accident.]] Later that night, Logan was sleeping in his room, having a nightmare about when the adamantium was attached to his skeleton. Rogue entered and tried to wake him up, but as he awoke, still tense from the nightmare, he screamed and his claws unsheathed in her chest. Shocked, he retracted them, leaving three holes in her body, and she quivered. He started yelling for help hysterically and she put up her hand and touched his cheek. Ororo, Jean, Scott and some kids (including Bobby) entered just in time to see her wounds heal and Logan pass out. Jean and Scott rushed to help Logan, and Rogue started apologizing and saying that it was an accident. She left the room and the other children stepped away from her in a panic. They looked at her with apprehension, though [[Iceman]] just looked concerned. Later, Logan woke up with Xavier sitting next to him. He asked the professor if Rogue was fine, and he answers that she would be alright. Wolverine asked what happened and Xavier answered that whenever Rogue touched someone, she would absorb their energy. In the case of mutants, she would absorb their gifts. For Logan, she absorbed his ability to heal. Logan said that he felt like she almost killed him and Xavier said that if she had held on any longer, she could have. In the basement, Xavier and Cyclops were trying to figure out what Magneto wanted with Wolverine. Xavier picked up that Scott did not like Logan. Just then, Logan and Ororo walked in and Logan asked where Rogue was. Xavier, after a short mind scan, said that she was gone. A few minutes later, Xavier, Scott, Jean, Ororo and Logan entered a secured room and Xavier welcomed Logan to Cerebro. Cerebro was a big round room, with a bridge leading to the machine. Xavier explained that Cerebro would amplify his powers and allow him to track mutants through large distances, which is how he intended to find Rogue. Logan asked why he did not use it to find Magneto, and Xavier told him that Magneto somehow found a way to shield himself from the machine. Logan asked how he would know how to do that and Xavier responded, saying that Magneto helped him build it. Then, he put the Cerebro helmet on his head and, after the others exit, started the search. Outside, Logan asked Jean if she had ever used Cerebro, and she answered she had not because it took a certain degree of control and for someone like her it would be dangerous. Inside, Xavier managed to locate Rogue at the train station after a short search. Logan started leaving to find her but Xavier stopped him, saying that if he left the mansion Magneto would capture him. Instead, he sent Cyclops and Storm to find her. In the garage, Scott and Ororo prepared to leave and Scott noticed that his motorcycle was missing. On the road, Logan was riding Scott's motorcycle to the train station, where Rogue was sitting on a train, waiting for it to leave. She watched other people touching each other freely and felt sad that she would never get to feel human touch again. Logan found her and sat next to her. They apologized to each other about what had happened the previous night, and he asked her if she was running again. She told him that a boy in school told her that the professor was mad at her. While on the train, Rogue asked Wolverine if he thought she should go back. He answered that he thought she should follow her instincts. After a pause, she told him about the first boy she had ever kissed, [[David]], who had ended up in a coma for three weeks because of the kiss, and that she could still feel him and Logan inside her head. Logan hugged her through her shirt and convinced Rogue to stay with Xavier by telling her that he thought Xavier was one of the few people who could understand what she was going through and that he really wanted to help her, thus promising to take care of her. The train started to move and Logan convinced her to give "these geeks" another shot. [[File:XMENSTILL-002.jpg|left|thumb|250px|Wolverine tries to protect Rogue and is stopped by Magneto.]] When the [[Brotherhood of Mutants]] arrived at the train station, Rogue's train stopped moving and the lights went out. Everybody screamed as Wolverine looked in horror as Magneto used his powers to literally slice the train up open and floated in. Logan popped his claws and Magneto said that he must be Wolverine, the man with metal running through all his body. He used his powers to lift Logan in the air and inflicted pain on him as Rogue screamed at him to stop. Logan asked Magneto what he wanted from him and Magneto answered that it wasn't him he wanted. Logan was horrified to realize that Magneto was really after Rogue. Magneto incapacitated Wolverine by tossing him away and then knocked Rogue unconscious with a tranquilizer dart and captured her. She soon became a component in Magneto's machine on the Statue of Liberty. Back at the mansion, Logan was washing his face. He was angry at the professor for saying Magneto wanted him, and Xavier apologized, saying he had made a terrible mistake and Magneto's helmet was somehow designed to stop his telepathy. Logan left angrily to find her alone, but Storm tried to convince him to help and fight with them to defend the humans from Magneto. Logan declined, saying he did not want to help a world that hated him. He then said that Magneto was right – there was a war coming – and asked Storm if she was sure she was on the right side. She answered that at least she had chosen a side. He opened the door to leave and found [[Senator Kelly]] standing on the other side, looking for Dr. Jean Grey. [[File:X-men-movie-screencaps.com-7315.jpg|thumb|250px|Logan discovers that Magneto is using Rogue to power the device.]] Underground, Kelly was lying on a table. Logan and Jean were standing there too. Xavier approached him and introduced himself. Kelly said that he was afraid to go to a regular hospital, and Xavier completed his sentence, saying that he was afraid they would treat him like a mutant. He told him that they were not all dangerous like he thought and Kelly told him to tell that to the ones who did this to him. Xavier asked Kelly to relax and started reading his mind. He entered his memory of Magneto turning him into a mutant and Mystique helping him out of the machine. Magneto looked tired and drained from the process. Back in reality, a short while later, Xavier, Scott, Jean and Logan were in a room upstairs. Xavier explained that Magneto used a machine that produced radiation that triggered mutation in ordinary human beings. Jean continued, saying that the mutation was unnatural and Kelly's body was rejecting it – his cells were breaking down. Scott asked what the radiation will do to mutants and Xavier answered that nothing would happen, but it could seriously harm humans. Logan asked what Magneto wanted with Rogue and Xavier answered that he does not know. Annoyed, Logan turned to leave again, but then Scott found the answer – the machine had drawn its power from Magneto and it had nearly killed him. He deduced that Magneto wanted Rogue to absorb his power so she could do it in his place, which would allow him live. Storm entered Xavier's study just in time to break up an argument between Scott and Logan and informed them that Senator Kelly was dead. Xavier told them that he would go find Rogue and ordered the men to settle their dispute. Cyclops, Jean, Storm and Wolverine discussed their battle plans. They understood Magneto's plan now completely, and they knew Magneto was not aware of the fact that his machine would kill regular humans. They knew that if he gave Rogue enough power, he would kill everybody in New York City. They planned to land on Liberty Island and spoil his plans. Logan asked about harbour patrol and Scott answered that if they had anything that could find their jet, they deserved to catch them. They left in the Blackbird as the students watched in awe. Scott flew the jet and when they reached the bridge, they landed on the water with Storm creating fog as cover. The Blackbird landed and the X-Men got out. They look up, and saw the machine on top of the torch. As they passed through the metal detector, it beeped on Logan but he popped in claws in it. Cyclops turned towards him with an annoyed expression, and Wolverine sheathed the two outer claws, essentially flipping him off. Scott smiled and Jean rolled her eyes. They started walking carefully down the hall, but Toad and Mystique were already waiting for them, hidden. Logan's senses picked up on them, and he tells the others that there was someone there but he did not know where, and said to "keep your eye open" as a joke on Scott's expense. Out of nowhere, another Wolverine showed up and started battling Logan. The others weren't sure anymore who was the real Logan and who was the imposter. They both popped their claws and started attacking each other, slicing up the place in the process. Eventually, Mystique shifted back to her true form and managed to outmanoeuvre Wolverine. [[File:X-men-movie-screencaps.com-9180.jpg|left|thumb|250px|Wolverine outsmarts Mystique and defeats her.]] Later on, Storm approached Logan and asked if it was him. He silenced her, used his sense of smell and told her that the other one was not far away. She said that they had to regroup and he responded, saying that there was a problem – she was not part of the group - and then unsheathed his claws in her stomach. She morphed to Mystique, and then fell to the ground. He found Scott and Jean, and Scott told him to prove that it really was him, to which Logan simply answered, "you're a dick", which Scott thought was proof enough. Storm showed up upstairs and they followed her up. They saw the torch and Logan suddenly told them all to get out of there. Cyclops asked what the problem was, and Logan said that he could not move. He floated in the air and gets pinned to the wall. Metal pieces flew and hooked the others to the walls, too, despite Scott's attempts to blast them. Cyclops was hooked in a way that his eyes, covered by his visor, was pointed at Jean's head, preventing him from shooting. Magneto floated down and aimed Logan's hands to his chest, which prevented him from popping his claws. Sabretooth joined them and removed Scott's visor, forcing him to close his eyes. Scott ordered Storm to fry him, but Magneto reminded him that it was not a good idea to send a bolted lightning into a huge conductive metal like copper, which the Statue of Liberty was made of. Magneto explained his motives to the X-Men and they heard Rogue crying for help. Wolverine told him that if he were really so righteous, it would have been him in the machine. Magneto flew up to the machine, as Rogue continued to scream for help. Logan used all his strength to release himself with the claws, seemingly hurting himself and Jean yelled his name in worry. Sabretooth approached his body and Logan leaped at him and popped his claws into his stomach. Sabretooth shared Wolverine's healing factor, though, and they started battling on the statue's head. During the battle, Wolverine recovered his dog tags. Sabretooth threw Wolverine off the statue, but he managed to cling on and jump up. He beat Sabretooth and heard Rogue scream in pain. He fell down and jammed his claws to the wall to hang on it, almost killing Scott and Jean, who were at the other side of the wall. Sabretooth thought he was dead and walked to Storm, only for Wolverine to jump back in with Cyclops' visor. Jean told Scott that when she told him, he should open his eyes. The torch smashed and the machine began to work. Jean telekinetically placed the visor on Scott's eyes and said "Now!", and he shot, blasting Sabretooth through the wall and crashed into a boat anchored off Liberty Island as Wolverine released the others. He asked Cyclops if he could blast the machine, but Scott said that he would kill Rogue. Cyclops asked Storm if she could lift him up, but she said that she could not control it like that – he could fly right over the torch. Logan told her to levitate him – if he does not make it, at least Cyclops could still blast it. Cyclops agreed and ordered Jean to try to steady Logan with her telekinesis. With combined efforts, the two managed to direct Logan to the top of the machine. The energy field started to form, and Wolverine jumped inside. He tried to slash Rogue's chains but Magneto stopped him with his power. Cyclops put his hand to his visor, but Jean asked him to wait. [[File:Wolverine claws bend.jpg|right|thumb|250px|Wolverine on Magneto's machine.]] The X-Men ultimately saved her before the machine could stretch Magneto's power any further as Cyclops saw a chance and decided to take it, managing to blast Magneto. Free to move, Wolverine slashed the machine, stopping the field before it mutated anyone. He cut Rogue's chains and held her in his arms, her being weakened by the machine. She wasn't moving and he noticed the white streak in her hair. He touched her cheek, attempting to give her his regenerative powers, trying to get her to heal in order to save her life. At first, it didn't seem to work and Logan began to despair, thinking Rogue to be dead. Suddenly, a multitude of wounds began to appear across his face and blood dripped out of several stab wounds. Rogue suddenly woke up and Logan fell to the ground, unconscious, with gashes all over his face and chest. Back in the mansion, Logan awoke to find Jean standing by him. He asked her how they did, and she showed him that Xavier was lying on the surgical bench next to his. Later, she checked on Logan and found that all his wounds had healed. He woke up and she told him that what he did was very brave. He asked if it worked and she said that it did, that Rogue's fine. He began to tell her how he felt about her, but her reaction made it evident that, while Logan's heart may have belonged to her, hers belonged to Scott. Logan changed the subject, instead asking how the professor was. She told him that he was fine. Later, the professor told Logan that there was an abandoned military compound in the Canadian Rockies close to where they found him, and that he might find some answers there. He asked Logan if he was going to say goodbye to the kids. Rogue spotted Logan leaving and rushed to talk to him. He told her that he had some things to take care of in the north. She said that she didn't want him to go, and he said that he would be back, leaving her with his dog tags, something to remember him by when he returned. Wolverine then left the mansion on Cyclops' motorcycle, heading to Alkali Lake. ===Rematch with Sabretooth=== Logan stopped to fill [[Cyclops]]' motorcycle with gas and found that [[Sabretooth]] had been following him. They fought, which ended with Logan defeating him; however, before he killed him, Logan found that Sabretooth had the same dog tags as him. The two go for drinks and started having a conversation. Stryker's men came in with the orders from Stryker and aimed at Sabretooth. [[File:B3F2EA76-5BBA-44EA-AFE2-CB634F784DD9.jpeg|thumb|250px|Logan faces off against Sabretooth again]] The two fought the soldiers with great teamwork, not knowing they were brothers and were eventually brought down. Waking up in a helicopter, Sabretooth threw Wolverine off, sacrificing himself in the process. Stryker later found out that the adamantium bonding did not work with Sabretooth and ordered his men to shoot Victor with an adamantium bullet, killing him, while Logan continued his search for Alkali Lake. ===Stopping William Stryker=== {{Quote|You cut me open. You took my life.|Wolverine|X2}} [[File:Logan at Alkali Lake (X2 - 2003).png|left|thumb|250px|Wolverine at Alkali Lake.]] ﻿At Alkali Lake, in Canada, Wolverine was walking in the snow. He saw the military compound that Professor X was talking about. In the gate, he spotted a white wolf. He entered but found that nothing was left but the walls that once surrounded the structure. It was now abandoned and demolished to his disappointment. He then returned to the X-Mansion. Rogue heard Cyclops's motorcycle and happily ran to the entrance to see Logan returning home. They hugged and greeted each other, and Rogue introduced Bobby to Logan as her boyfriend. They shook hands and Bobby told him to call him Iceman as he slightly froze Logan's hand. Logan then started teasing the two on how they got along physically. Bobby answered that they were still working on that. Suddenly, Ororo came down the stairs and welcomed Logan home, saying that he had come just in time – they needed a babysitter. Jean came down after her and greeted Logan while Storm left to get the jet ready. Bobby and Rogue also left. Jean explained to Logan that she and Storm were heading to Boston and said that they wouldn't be gone long – the Professor wanted them to track down a mutant who had attacked the [[President McKenna|President]]. He started flirting with her and Scott walked down the staircase, asking him if he found what he was looking for. Logan answered him and Jean kissed Scott goodbye, leaving towards the Hangar Bay. With Jean gone, Logan told Scott that his bike needed gas, tossing him the keys. Scott replied that Wolverine could fill it up himself, before tossing the keys back to him. In Cerebro, the Professor was getting ready to find the mutant attacker. Wolverine entered and the Professor welcomed him back before telling him to put out his cigar. Logan, looking for a place to put it out, finally decided to put it out on his palm, creating a wound that quickly healed. He asked Xavier if he wanted him to leave while Xavier used Cerebro. Xavier told him to stand still. Everything got dark and millions of dotted lights appeared. Xavier explained that these lights represented every living person on the world – the white lights were human, the red were the mutants – and that through Cerebro, he was connected to them and them to him. Logan changed the subject and said that he found the base in Alkali Lake, but there was nothing there. Xavier, seemingly ignoring Logan, said that the broken line represented the mutant who had attacked the President and Xavier had been having a hard time getting a lock on him. Logan asked if he could just concentrate harder and Xavier answered that he could, but it would kill the mutant they were trying to find. Just then, the mutant stopped running and his voice was heard praying in German. Xavier left Cerebro and Logan asked him to read his mind again. Xavier said that the results would be the same as before, and that the mind was not a box that could simply be unlocked and opened. He had no doubt that Logan's amnesia, his adamantium skeleton, and the claws were all connected, but sometimes the mind needed to discover things for itself. Before wheeling out, he asked Logan to watch over the kids that night as he and Scott went to visit an old friend. In the Institute, Logan was having another nightmare about his past and about what Xavier told him earlier in Cerebro and awoke, sweaty. He walked downstairs and heard the TV is on. One of the students, [[Jones]], was sitting in front of the television and changed channels just by blinking. Jones asked him if he couldn't sleep, and Logan jokingly asked "How can you tell?", to which the kid simply answered – "'Cause you're awake." Wolverine asked him the same question, and Jones answered that he doesn't sleep. Logan then walked to the kitchen and found Bobby eating ice-cream. He opened the fridge and asked him if they had any beer, to which Bobby answered that it was a school. Wolverine asked if that was a no and Bobby said that it was. He asked if they had anything other than chocolate milk and Bobby answered that they had soda. Logan took a bottle of soda and handed it to Bobby, who softly blew ice into the bottle to cool it before Logan drank it. Logan asked Bobby how long he had been at the mansion. Bobby answered, "A couple of years." He asked if Bobby's parents just sent him off to mutant school. He answered that they thought he was attending a prep school. Logan changed the subject to Bobby's relationship with Rogue and Bobby told him that it was hard because he wanted to be physically close to her, but couldn't be. All of a sudden, during their conversation, Logan's acute senses picked up on intruders' footsteps. He got up and hushed Bobby. Logan left the kitchen just as a soldier sneaked in, almost shooting Bobby from behind. Logan grabbed him out in time. [[Siryn]], a sleeping student awoken by the intruders, screamed at the sight of the soldiers and released a strong sonic scream heard throughout the school, shattering all the windows, waking up the other students, and making everyone cover their ears with their hands. The soldier in the kitchen tried firing all over the place but Bobby ducked in time. Logan went berserk and popped his claws in the soldier's chest, killing him in front of Bobby. Logan and Bobby left the kitchen and spotted some soldiers. Logan told Bobby to stay put and took out the soldiers. Logan kept taking out soldiers as Bobby ran to the elevator to go and find Rogue. [[File:45 (2).jpg|right|thumb|250px|Wolverine defends the X-Mansion against intruders.]] Logan found [[Peter Rasputin]] and handed him Jones, who Logan found unconscious. Peter said he could help Logan, but he told Peter to help the kids. Piotr and Jones entered the hallway and Logan surprised more soldiers. Soldiers ran in front of Iceman, Rogue, and [[Pyro|Pyro/John]], but Logan jumped at them and quickly took them out with his claws. They ran from some soldiers and entered an elevator but Logan left right before it closed to confront the soldiers. Suddenly, Stryker's voice was heard, telling the soldiers not to shoot him yet. Stryker called him by his codename and said that the mansion was the last place he expected to find Logan in, as he hadn't seen Logan in over 15 years. Stryker, himself surprised to see Wolverine - the man he had worked with, experimented on, and severely injured decades earlier - in the mansion, revealed that he knew much about Wolverine. Logan didn't remember Stryker and asked him who he was. Before Stryker could answer, though, a thick shield of ice separated them and Rogue and Bobby called Logan over. Wanting to get answers from Stryker, he told them to go, that he would be fine, but Rogue responded, saying that they wouldn't be. Stryker drilled through the ice, but by then Logan and the kids were already heading to the garage and getting into Cyclops's car. Pyro wanted to drive but Logan drove instead. Rogue was in the passenger seat and the boys sat in the back. Logan started the car with his claws and they took off. The soldiers, meanwhile, got into the underground tunnels. On the road, Pyro asked what just happened and Logan answered that his name was Stryker, but he couldn't remember who he was. Pyro started the CD player but instead a communicator came out. Wolverine said that they should head to Boston, since that's where Storm and Jean were going. Bobby said that his parents lived in Boston. They decided they would go to Bobby's house to seek refuge, rest, and regain their bearings. While in the car, Rogue returned [[Wolverine's Dog Tag]], which was given to her before he left for Alkali Lake. In the Drake residence, Bobby and the others entered and Bobby called for his family. Nobody was home and Bobby told John not to burn anything as he went to find clothes for everyone. Downstairs, Logan tried to contact Jean through Scott's communicator but it didn't work. In the kitchen, Logan took a bottle of beer from the fridge, and, sensing something near him, popped his claws at the house cat. The cat wasn't scared and started licking Logan's claws. He heard the door open and retracted his claws as Bobby's parents and brother entered and found him. The father, [[William Drake]], asked him who he was as Bobby rushed down the stairs and introduced him as Professor Logan. He told his parents that there was something he needed to tell them. In the Drakes' living room, Bobby finished telling his parents and brother about his mutation. Pyro was playing with his lighter. Bobby's mother, [[Madeline Drake]], started asking her son when he first discovered he was a mutant, but, when she couldn't bring herself to say the word 'mutant', Pyro said it for her tauntingly. William Drake asked John to understand that they thought Bobby was going to a school for the gifted and Rogue told him that Bobby ''was'' gifted. Madeline told Bobby that they still loved him, but these mutant problems were complicated. Logan cut her off mid-sentence, asking, "What mutant problems?" William asked him what he was a professor of, and he answered, "Art." Rogue suggested that Bobby show his family what he could do, and Bobby froze his mother's cup of coffee, saying that he could do a lot more than that. Madeline was shocked and Pyro seemed rather amused by this whole conversation, almost mocking the parents' attitude. The demonstration was about all that Bobby's brother, Ronny, could take and he left the living room angrily. Madeline said that this was all her fault, but Pyro taunts the parents, saying that it had been discovered that males were the ones who carry the mutant gene and pass it on, so it was William's fault. Ronny, listening from upstairs, called the police. [[File:Image,ik.jpg|left|thumb|250px|Wolverine with Iceman, Pyro and Rogue.]] Cyclops' communicator beeped and Logan picked it up. On the porch, Logan tells Jean that there was no one left in the Institute since the soldiers had come. Storm asked about the children and Logan answered that some of them escaped, but he wasn't sure about the rest. Jean told him that they hadn't been able to reach the Professor or Scott either. He told them that he was in Boston with Bobby Drake's family and the women head there. He told them to make it fast and hung up. He went back inside and told the kids that they had to go now. In a hurry, they all stepped out, Logan's claws out, and found that the house was surrounded by police cars. An officer told him to drop the knives and to put his hands in the air. Logan asked what was going on and Bobby was frustrated to realize that his brother had called the police. From his bedroom window, Ronny was watching everything. On the porch, two officers broke in and told Bobby's parents to stand by the wall. Logan told the officers that this was just a misunderstanding. They ordered him again to put the knives down and he told them he couldn't. He put his hands up in front of an officer, trying to reason with him, but the frightened officer shot him in the forehead, and he dropped. While Wolverine was unconscious, John torched Bobby's house to the point where Rogue was forced to use her powers to stop him. The fire all around them stopped and he fell to his knees. Bobby watched as the wound on Logan's forehead healed and the bullet came out. Just then, the Blackbird landed in the yard and the mutants walked to it as none of the officers dared to shoot them. On the jet, the four mutants sat down. Wolverine, not recognizing a mutant on the jet, asked who the new guy was. [[Nightcrawler|Kurt Wagner/Nightcrawler]] answered and told him that in the Munich circus he had been known as the incredible Nightcrawler. They took off in a hurry, as Bobby was still shocked and sad at his parents' and brother's reactions. On the jet, Jean told Wolverine that they were close to the mansion. Just then, though, Storm detected two signals approaching fast. Two small military jets were tailing them and ordered them to descend in 10 seconds. Logan blamed John for the fact that they were angry. The jets gave their orders again and when Storm doesn't comply, they prepare to fire. Storm tried to manoeuvre and get away and her eyes began to glow white. The sky turned dark and small tornadoes appeared in the air. It worked with one of the jet and it dropped but the other one had managed to fire two torpedoes before the pilot jumped with his parachute. Storm stopped her power, but then spotted the torpedoes. This time it was Jean's turn to use her power, with her eyes glowing orange, and she got one of the torpedoes off track with her telekinesis. She couldn't get the other one in time, though, and it hit them, blowing a hole in the jet as they began to lose altitude. Rogue, who wasn't wearing her seatbelt, screamed as she was sucked through the hole created by the torpedo and fell out of jet. Bobby and Logan screamed, but it was Kurt who saved her, teleporting next to her, grabbing her, and teleporting her back inside. Still, the jet was falling rapidly until suddenly the hole suddenly closed by itself and the jet stopped mid-air, meters from the ground. Storm asked Jean if she was responsible for that, and she said that it wasn't her. On the ground, Magneto and Mystique were laughing, with Magneto humorously saying "When will these people learn how to fly?". That night, the mutants had set up camp. By the fire, Magneto told the X-Men that their foe was Colonel William Stryker and that he had invaded their mansion for one purpose – he wanted Cerebro, or enough of it to build one of his own. Jean said that that didn't make any sense because he would need the professor to operate it, and Magneto said that that was probably the only reason his old friend, Xavier, was still alive. Jean and Storm got worried, realizing the disaster potential, and Wolverine asked what they were so worried of. Magneto explained to him that when Cerebro was working, Charles's mind was connected to every living person on the planet – if he was forced to concentrate hard enough on a particular group, for example mutants, he could kill them all. Storm asked how Stryker knew where to find Cerebro in the first place and Magneto answered that he told him – Stryker had strong means of persuasion, even against a mutant as strong as Xavier. Jean wanted to know more about Stryker and Magneto said that he was a military scientist who had spent his whole life trying to solve "the mutant problem", and said that Wolverine had a more intimate perspective as his adamantium skeleton had been created by Stryker. Wolverine didn't understand why the professor hadn't told him, and Magneto said that Xavier had thought he was smart enough to discover it on his own – he gave him more credit than he deserved. Storm asked what he needed them for and Magneto said that Mystique had discovered plans of a base Stryker had been operating from for decades and that was where he was building the second Cerebro, but they didn't know where this base is and he believed one of them might know. Wolverine told him that the professor already tried, but Magneto said that he wasn't talking about him. He looked up to see Nightcrawler sitting on a branch above them. A few minutes later, Jean scanned Kurt's mind and saw horrible sights, ones very similar to the ones she had seen in Wolverine's mind, including an operation. After she finished, she said that Stryker was at Alkali Lake, but Logan said that that was where the professor had sent him and that there was nothing left. Jean explained that there was nothing left on the surface – the base was underground. Later, Logan and Jean were alone. He asked her how she was doing and she said she was worried about Scott. He told her that her power, which she had activated in the jet, was powerful, but she said that obviously it hadn't been enough. He put his hand on her but she said that she loved Scott – girls flirted with the dangerous guy (Logan), they don't take him home – they marry a good guy (Scott). He kissed her, but she stopped him after a few seconds, asking him not to make her do this, and left. [[File:Wolverine and Mystique.jpg|thumb|250px|Wolverine believes it's [[Mystique|Jean Grey]] who wants to keep him company.]] Kurt talked to Mystique and asked if it was true she was a shapeshifter. Mystique affirmed that she was a shapeshifter, but ultimately preferred remaining in her natural form when possible. Logan was looking down at his dog tag, when Jean suddenly entered and kissed him. After a few seconds of kissing her, Logan's face took on a suspicious look. He had felt the scars he caused on Mystique when he stabbed her. 'Jean' shifted back into Mystique and said to him, "No one's ever left a scar quite like you," while Logan sarcastically replied, "What do you want, an apology?". Mystique shifted a few more times to Jean, Rogue, and, finally, Ororo. Logan kicked Mystique back, angry. Mystique shifted to Stryker for a moment, then leaves. The following morning, they all sat in the jet, where they all take their uniforms. The kids complained that they didn't get uniforms and Logan said that theirs were on order – they would arrive in a few years. Outside in the jet, the X-Men were planning the operation using a holographic topographic map of the base. They saw that Stryker could easily flood the spillway if they went inside. Storm asked Nightcrawler if he could teleport them in, but he said he has to see where he was teleporting or he might end up in a wall. Wolverine offered to go, hazarding a guess that Stryker wanted him alive. Magneto said that whoever entered has to know how to operate the place, and looked at Mystique. [[File:45 (1).jpg|left|thumb|250px|Wolverine gains memories at Alkali Lake.]] At Alkali Lake, Jean discovered that Stryker's base was underground. Mystique, impersonating Wolverine, infiltrated the base and tricked Stryker's troops into opening a gateway for the X-Men to enter. In the control room, Mystique watched as the doors explode. It wasn't the soldiers, though, but the mutants, and they entered. She showed them a map of the base, highlighting a large round room – Cerebro. Logan, standing apart from the others, saw Stryker on a screen. He heard the other planning to part – Storm and Nightcrawler would get the kids while Jean, Magneto, and Mystique would go to shut down Cerebro. Storm noticed that Logan was gone. [[File:X-men-2-2003-187-g.jpg|thumb|250px|Wolverine fights Lady Deathstrike at Alkali Lake.]] Wolverine walked down a stairway and found the room of his nightmares – what seemed to be a surgery room. In the middle, there was a green tube of liquid of some sort. On the walls were X-ray pictures of skulls and claws. Logan had another flash of past memories of himself in this room lying in the tube. He kept looking around and found a small bathtub-like pool of liquid adamantium. Next to it, there were needles and he got a flash of the operation where his skeleton had been cased adamantium. He looked at the tube and saw a flash of himself running away from it, naked, and seeing his claws for the first time. Stryker and [[Lady Deathstrike|Lady Deathstryke/Yuriko]] entered the room and Stryker started explaining to him about how he had to keep the adamantium hot and liquid because once it cooled, it was indestructible. Stryker then left and told Wolverine that he used to think that he was one of a kind, but he was wrong. Logan ran after him, but Yuriko blocked his exit and tossed him across the room. He popped his claws and watched in shock as she pulled out adamantium claws of her own – five, one from each fingertip. She leaped at him, slicing him across the face, and they started fighting each other, with her having the upper hand. When she threw him at a wall and ran to him, he surprised her and brought his claws to her stomach, earning him the opportunity for a few more strikes before she could respond. She had a healing factor just as good as his own, though, and recovered quickly. Again, she tossed him across the room and stabbed him in the back several times before tossing him on an iron net platform hanging above the tube. She stood below him and stabbed him repeatedly through the net, in his back and neck until he used his claws to destroy the poles that the platform was hanged from, and it falls on her, locking her in the tube. Wolverine was still recovering from his injuries when Lady Deathstryke suddenly jumped at him from below and stabbed him again. He spotted the needles by the adamantium and managed to grab one of them, injecting her with molten, liquefied adamantium metal. She immediately stopped moving, and, while she was impaled by Wolverine, broke free of Stryker's mind control, but there was now nothing she could do to save herself. Wolverine watched in horror as adamantium started leaking from her eyes, nose, and ears, until she fell backwards into the water tank with a thud as the metal hardened in her body. He sighed in relief. Outside, Stryker took the cables keeping the helicopter on the ground off, but Wolverine showed up, punching him and popping his claws in his arm. Logan yelled at him for experimenting on him and stealing his life, but Stryker told him that he volunteered for the procedure willingly. Wanting answers, Logan asked Stryker who he was, but Stryker told him that he was nothing but a failed experiment, provoking Logan into digging his claws deeper into Stryker's arm. Stryker didn't want the mutant to remember the work they had done together – he had been an animal then, he was an animal now, Stryker said, he just gave him claws. Suddenly, they heard an alarm going off, then water burst out of the dam. Logan asked Stryker what was happening and Stryker told him that the spillway was about to get flooded because the pressure had overflowed. In an attempt to escape alive, Stryker said that it was too late for anyone who was inside to make it out in time and offered to take Logan with him, and he could get all the answers he wanted. Wolverine repeated Stryker's former words, saying that he was just an animal with claws – if they die, he dies. He then popped his claws in Stryker's neck and tied him to the helicopter's leg with the cable before running back in. As the dam kept breaking, shaking the whole structure, Jean tried scanning for the only missing person – Logan. Logan was running through the halls, desperate to save his friends. After saving Cyclops and Professor X, all the mutants ran through the halls to the spillway, believing it to be their only escape. They got to the doors but Logan came out of a tunnel and popped his claws in a fuse box, closing the door before they got caught in the flood. He told them that they didn't want to go that way and water burst through the cracks. Logan led his friends through the other way out. Outside, the water stopped leaking through the dam as it had been redirected. The mutants rushed out onto the snow. [[Artie Maddicks|Artie]] fell, and Logan carried him in his arms. To their shock and horror, the helicopter was already gone, taken by Magneto. Suddenly, the jet showed up spinning uncontrollably in the air. Rogue was piloting and made an emergency landing, crashing in the snow. Both her and Bobby seemed scared to death and were breathing heavily as their friends entered the jet. Kurt teleported Xavier inside while Logan, still carrying Artie, looked at the woods and went in them instead of to the jet. In the woods, Logan came face to face with Stryker, who was tied to a concrete wall by chains, one last time. Stryker said, "Who has the answers, Wolverine? Those people? That creature in your arms?" Looking at Artie, Wolverine put him down, dropped the dog tags Stryker fashioned for him years earlier, and informed Stryker that he would "take his chances with him," and left Stryker to die, leaving him at the mercy of the collapsing dam. As Wolverine walked away, Stryker bellowed after him, "One day! Someone will finish what I've started Wolverine! One day! One day!" Artie stuck out his blue tongue as a reply. Wolverine and Artie Maddicks were the last two people to see Stryker. Wolverine entered the jet and handed Artie to Bobby. Jean asked him if he was okay and he said that he was now. Scott and Ororo started panicking, realizing that something was wrong and they couldn't take off. Rogue asked if anyone had seen John and Jean answered after a short telepathic scan that he had joined Magneto, to everyone's disappointment. The jet had lost power and the water burst out through the dam again, which was unable to handling the building pressure. Jean, in the back, realized she that she had to do something as she was their last hope. She looked at all her friends and left the jet without them noticing. After a few seconds, though, Xavier notices and called her name, alarming Logan and Scott. Xavier said that she was outside and Scott and Logan rushed to the exit. Jean telekinetically closed the door, keeping them inside, and activated the jet. Scott yelled at Ororo not to take off, but Jean does it for her anyway. Jean used one hand to keep the water from drowning the jet and the other to free the jet's leg from the snow. Scott watched in misery and Logan ordered Kurt to get her, but Kurt said that she wasn't letting him. As her last words of goodbye, Jean used her telepathy to speak through Xavier's mouth, saying she that she knew what she was doing – this was the only way. Scott begged her not to do this but she said goodbye. Her body and eyes glowed orange as she manages to release the jet's leg and lift it into the air. She released her telekinetic grip, letting the water drown her and everybody grieved for her. Scott fell, crying, onto Logan. Everybody was overwhelmed, their throats choking with sorrow. The jet flew above what was now a large lake. In Washington, Professor X had frozen everyone in [[President McKenna]]'s office and the X-Men infiltrate it right before the president was about to make a presidential address. The sky turned dark and a bolt of lightning struck, lighting up the room to reveal the mutants standing in front of the president with Storm's eyes glowing white. The president was scared to see his attacker, Nightcrawler, among them. Professor X greeted the president and calmed him down, saying that they wouldn't harm anyone. The president asked who they were and Xavier answered that they were mutants, and his name was Charles Xavier. Rogue put some documents down on the president's desk, and Xavier explained that they had been taken from the private office of William Stryker. The president asked how they got it, and the professor answered that he knew a little girl who could walk through walls. The president, after looking through the documents, said that he had never seen this information and Xavier responded, saying that he knew that. The president said that he didn't respond well to threats and Xavier said that this was not a threat – this was an opportunity. There were forces in this world, he said, both human and mutant alike, who believed that a war was coming. As he could see from the files, some had already tried to start one, and there had been casualties – losses, on both sides. He ended by telling the president that what he was about to tell the world in the presidential address was true: this was an important moment – a moment to repeat the mistakes of the past or to work together for a better future. Mutants were there to stay – the next move was his. Logan said that they would be watching as a bolt of lightning brightens all their faces and they disappear. All the lights turn on again. The president was silent, not sure what to tell the cameras. In Xavier's study, Scott was looking out the window. Xavier said that even when Jean was a student, she was hesitant about her powers, always looking to others, fearing that in some way, she had been left behind. Scott asked in a choked voice if they could have done more to save her, and Xavier said that in the past, she may have let them. Logan couldn't understand why she left the plane and Xavier said that she made a choice. Students walked into the classroom, including Artie, Jones, Bobby, Moonstar, and Jubilee. As Logan and Scot leave the room, Logan told Scott that Jean ''did'' make a choice – it was Scott, referring to her and Scott's romantic relationship and her rejection of Logan. Scott walked away, evidently not finding much comfort in what Logan had said. ===Encountering Phoenix=== {{Quote|Sometimes, when you cage the beast, the beast gets angry.|Wolverine to [[Professor X]]|X-Men: The Last Stand}} [[File:Dangerroomf.jpg|left|thumb|250px|Wolverine in the Danger Room.]] Some time later, some of the X-Men and the students were fighting in an apocalyptic battle. Enemy fire blasted buildings around them, and everything was in flames. The students were running through the chaos and Storm flew through the air, avoiding getting hit. Rogue and Colossus were about to get hit by a huge flying rock but Peter turned to steel and stood in its way, sending the rock flying at Wolverine's direction, hitting him in the head. Logan simply healed himself and looked at his students in disappointment – the world was turning to hell and they were pretty much sitting around. He called to them to follow him and they run after him. Logan nonchalantly used a burning log to flame his cigar just as Ororo rushed to him in a panic, telling him that the students were getting killed out there. He agreed, saying that they weren't ready. Ororo pushed him away at the last moment before getting blasted themselves, and he got annoyed that he had lost his last cigar. They realized that the enemy was flying straight at them at high speeds from the sky, and Logan gave up on his students. He popped his claws and called to Colossus, telling him to throw him at the enemy. Colossus turned to steel again and tossed Wolverine at the enemy despite Storm's objections. A big clash was heard and a destroyed Sentinel head fell from the sky with Logan on it. The enemy disengaged and Wolverine dismissed the class. The Danger Room returned to normal as Logan complimented Peter on his throw. As they exited the Danger Room, Storm angrily told Wolverine that he couldn't just change the rules – she was trying to teach them something. Logan defended himself, saying that he taught them something, but Ororo stated that it was meant to be a defensive exercise. He answered that the best defense was a good offense – or was it the other way around? She told him that it wasn't a game, but he tells her to go to Scott if she had a problem. In the hallway, Scott walked past Logan. Wolverine told Cyclops that they were waiting for him downstairs but Cyclops never showed up. Scott asked why he cared, and Logan answered that he had had to cover for him. Scott said that he hadn't asked him to, but Wolverine told him that the professor did. Cyclops tried to brush Logan off and walk away, but Logan helf him and said he knew how Scott felt about Jean's death, but maybe it was time for them to move on. Scott just walked away, telling Logan that not everybody could heal as fast as him. Later, when [[Hank McCoy]] had arrived at the X-Mansion and reunited with Storm and Professor X, Logan walked in and asked, "Who's the furball?" Hank introduced himself as the Secretary of Mutant Affairs, and Logan sarcastically complimented him on his suit. Xavier introduced Logan to Hank but Beast had already heard of Wolverine. Storm interrupted and said that Magneto would definitely come to get Mystique, but Beast said that Magneto was not the problem – at least not the most pressing one. He told the X-Men that a major pharmaceutical company had developed a mutant antibody – a way to suppress the mutant X-gene, permanently. They were calling it a "cure." For a moment, everybody was quiet, taking in the news, until Storm brole the silence, saying that that was ridiculous – they couldn't "cure" being a mutant – and since when had they become a disease? Xavier stopped her, sensing that the company was announcing it now. In the other room, Storm asked, "Who would want this cure? What kind of coward will take it just to fit in?" Beast asked if it was cowardice to save oneself from persecution, reminding her that not all of them could fit in so easily. Wolverine suggested that the government helped cook this up but Beast assured him that the government had nothing to do with it. Logan argued that he had heard that before and Hank angrily told him he had been fighting for mutant rights since before Logan had claws. Rogue walked in abruptly and interrupted their argument, asking if it was true that they could cure them. Xavier admitted that it appeared to be true, but Storm refused to accept it, saying that they couldn't cure them because there was nothing to cure. There was nothing wrong with Rogue, or any of them for that matter. [[File:Wolveirne_and_storm_run.jpg|thumb|250px|Wolverine and [[Storm]] run to [[Professor X]]'s aid.]] In Xavier's School, the professor sensed Cyclops' distress. Telepathically, Scott's name was heard all over the school. Something iswas clearly horribly wrong. Wolverine and Storm ran through the halls to Xavier's office, not knowing what just happened. They burst through the door and asked if he was okay and he told them to get to Alkali Lake. Back in Alkali Lake a short while later, the Blackbird descended into the heavy fog and lands. Wolverine and Storm stepped out carefully and started walking around, though none of them really wanted to be there. Logan said that he couldn't see a damn thing, but Ororo said that she could take care of that. Her eyes began to glow white and the thick fog faded away. In front of their eyes, the two X-Men were shocked to see small rocks, leaves, and drops of water floating around in the air. They split up to explore and Logan found something else: a pair of glasses floating mid-air.
