He grabbed them and realized that it was Scott's ruby glasses, but before he could do anything else, Ororo's scream caught his attention and he rushed to her. He found Storm sitting on the ground, near Jean Grey's still-alive body. Later in the school's infirmary, Logan and Professor X were at Jean's side. Xavier told Logan that the sheer mass of the water that had collapsed on top of Jean should have obliterated her completely. The only explanation of her survival was that her powers wrapped her up in a cocoon of telekinetic energy. Logan asked if she would be okay and Xavier said that Jean Grey was the only Class 5 mutant he had ever encountered – her potential was practically limitless. Her mutation was seeded in the unconscious part of her mind, posing a danger. When she was a girl, Xavier had built several psychic barriers in her mind to isolate her powers from her conscious mind, and as a result, Jean had developed a dual personality: the conscious Jean, whose powers were always in her control, and the dormant side, a personality that came to call itself "The Phoenix," a purely instinctual creature. Taking this in, Wolverine asked Xavier if Jean knew all this. Xavier answered that it was unclear how much she knew, but far more critical is whether the woman in front of them was the Jean Grey they know or the Phoenix, furiously struggling to be free. Logan said she looks pretty peaceful to him, but Xavier said that that was because he was keeping her that way, trying to restore the psychic blocks to cage the beast again. Thinking that this was too much, Logan angrily asked the professor what he had done to Jean – "We are talking about a person's mind here!" Xavier asked Wolverine to understand that Jean had to be controlled, but Logan told him that sometimes when you cage the beast, the beast gets angry. The professor told Logan he had no idea what Jean was capable of, but Logan corrected him, saying that he had no idea what the professor was capable of. Xavier explained that he had a terrible choice to make and that he had chosen the lesser of two evils. Logan answered that it sounded to him like Jean had no choice at all. Charles gave up and said that he didn't have to explain himself – least of all to Wolverine – and continued to work on Jean's mind. Angry, Logan walked out. [[File:Wolverine and Jean.jpg|left|thumb|250px|Logan and Jean kiss before she escapes.]] In the school's infirmary, Logan stood at Jean's side. Some sort of mental helmet was attached to her head, reading her brain waves. He touched her shoulder softly and she suddenly moved her hand sharply and grabbed his hand. She removed the helmet telekinetically and opened her eyes slowly. He called her name and welcomed her back and she commented that they were where they first met – only he was in her place and she was in his. Logan asked if she was okay and she said that she was more than okay. Jean slowly sat up and removed all medical instruments from herself. Jean smiled and told Logan he was making her blush and he asked if she was reading his thoughts. She sat up on the table and leaned closer to him, telling him that it was okay. They then started kissing. Jean did not appear to be quite her normal self, and her face took on a very forceful expression. Several open wounds started appearing on Logan's body out of nowhere. Logan was obviously in great pain, but Jean scratched his arm painfully, which causes a large gash. Worried, Logan stopped and said, "Wait." Jean's forceful expression did not leave, and she does not tone it down. It was obvious now that Jean was causing the gashes to appear all over Logan's body with her telekinetic powers. Logan jumped off the table, telling Jean that this wasn't her, but she sat up and says it was. Logan was now thoroughly concerned. He backed away from her and suggested she take it easy, saying the Professor had said she might be a little different. Hearing Xavier's name, Jean turned cold and sarcastically said the professor would know, suggesting he was in their heads all the time. She told Logan that Xavier had tamed him, but Logan realized that the professor was right and that the woman in front of him was not the Jean he knew. Now very worried about Cyclops' safety, Logan asked Jean, "Where's Scott?" For a moment, Jean snapped back to her usual self, asking Logan where she was. Logan told her she was in the mansion and said he needs to know what happened to Scott. Confused, Jean remained silent and just looked around the room. Logan held out Scott's glasses and repeated the question. Seeing the glasses, Jean started to remember how she had come out of the water earlier and saw Scott. She closed her eyes, and, suddenly, the room began to shake and the glasses broke to pieces in Logan's hand. Jean's face remained confused, with dazed stare, as the entire room shook violently. Logan grabbed Jean and told her to stay with him and talk to him. He yelled at her to look at him and focus, but she closes her eyes, her mind tortured by the personality struggle in her head. She then cried and begged Logan to kill her before she killed someone else, but he didn't want to hear it. He told her they could help her – the professor could fix it; he could help. This suggestion made Jean snap back to her previous state and she says that she didn't want to fix it, tossing Logan at the wall forcefully. With Wolverine unconscious, the Phoenix got up from the table, tore the massive, steel doors open telekinetically, and walked out. Storm and Professor X rushed in to find Wolverine still unconscious on the floor. Ororo woke Logan and asked him what happened, and he asks where Jean was. Worried that his greatest fear had come true, Xavier asked Logan what he had done. Logan told them that he thought Jean killed Scott, but Storm said that that was not possible. Furious, Xavier told Logan that he warned him and tried to scan for Jean telepathically. He found that she had left the mansion and was trying to block his thoughts. "She's so strong," he tells them, "It may be too late." [[File:Storm_&_Wolverine_(The_Last_Stand).png|thumb|250px|Storm and Wolverine prepare to fight [[Juggernaut]].]] Not long later, outside Grey residence, a car stopped. Out of it stepped the three X-Men, and Xavier told Logan and Ororo to wait for him outside – he needed to see Jean alone. Magneto showed up in front of them and told Charles he was right – this one was special. Wolverine asked Erik what he was doing there, and Magneto answered, "Same as the professor – visiting an old friend." Xavier told Erik that he didn't want trouble there, and Erik answered that neither did he. Magneto offered to go inside, and the two continued towards the house. Outside, the X-Men and the Brotherhood watched as the white gate shut close. Logan said that he was going in, but Ororo stopped him, reminding him the professor had told them to wait outside. Hearing the noise from outside, Logan decided that that was it and popped his claws, ready to fight his way in through the Brotherhood. Juggernaut responded by putting his helmet on and the two of them ran straight at each other. Storm used her wind powers to fly up to the sky, also preparing to battle. Juggernaut easily gained the upper hand, smashing Wolverine into the ground. He proceeded to grab Logan and toss him into the house through the front window. Logan landed on the dining table, smashing it to pieces. Juggernaut kept hitting and kicking Wolverine, tossing him around through the house, wrecking everything in his way. Juggernaut and [[Callisto]] continued to make quick work of the X-Men, ruining the house and smashing the walls. For a second, Wolverine managed to slash Juggernaut with his claws and distract him, but it didn't help much. Wolverine started using his claws to move around, sticking them into the ceiling and pulling himself towards the living room, struggling with Jean's telekinesis keeping him glued to the ceiling. He managed to get to the door and open it, arriving just in time to see Charles Xavier's whole body start to move as if his very skin was blowing in the wind like waves. Logan and Erik watched helplessly and in disbelief from opposite sides as Jean Grey used her powers on her former mentor. Suddenly, between all of the mess and noise and objects flying around, there was a moment of brief silent felt only by Jean and Xavier – as if time was moving in slow motion just for the two of them. Knowing that these were probably his last words, Charles Xavier smiled and told Jean not to let it control her. Then, in a second, the unbelievable happens – in front of Wolverine and Magneto's astonished eyes, Professor Charles Xavier's body blew up and vanished into thin air. After Magneto and Jean leave, Logan and Ororo ran into what used to be the living room but find only an empty wheelchair. They looked at each other with tears in their eyes and breathing heavily, realizing that their beloved mentor was gone. Logan dropped to his knees, helpless, yelling "No!" Ororo broke down in tears and fell on his back, hugging him. The two powerful X-Men were left alone in the wrecked house in front of an empty wheelchair, sobbing. The day after in Xavier's mansion, the wheelchair was resting in Xavier's study, orphaned of its owner. Outside on the front yard, Ororo Munroe was eulogizing Charles Xavier in a memorial service in front of all his students: "We live in an age of darkness," she said, "a world full of fear, hate and intolerance. But in every age, there are those who fight against it. Charles Xavier was born into a world divided, a world he tried to heal – a mission he never saw accomplished. It seems the destiny of great men to see their goals unfulfilled." In the crowd sat Marie D'Ancanto, Bobby Drake, Kitty Pryde, Peter Rasputin, Hank McCoy, and many other students and friends of Xavier's. Logan wasn't sitting with them, as he was standing and listening from afar, a loner as always. Rogue glanced at him as Ororo continued her eulogy: "Charles was more than a leader, more than a teacher, he was a friend. When we were afraid he gave us strength, and when we were alone he gave us a family. He may be gone, but his teachings live on through us, his students." Ororo finished, saying: "Wherever we may go, we must carry on his vision, and that's a vision of a world united." Logan walked away. All other people present stood up and one walked to his memorial and put a flower on it. Later, frustrated with her inability to touch her boyfriend, Rogue packed a bag and walked out of her room. On her way out, she was found by Logan, who asked her if she needed a lift. She said no, and he asked her where she was going. Thinking he would try to stop her, she started telling him he didn't know what it was like to be afraid of your own powers, to be afraid to get close to anybody. He told her that he did and she went on to explain she wanted to be able to touch people, to feel a hug, a handshake – a kiss. Logan said he hopes she wasn't doing it for some boy, and she remained silent, unsure of what to say. He told her that if she wanted to go, she could go – as long as she was sure it was what she wanted. Puzzled, Rogue asked if he wasn't supposed to tell her to go upstairs and unpack, but he explained that he wasn't her father – he was her friend. He told Rogue to think about what he said and she corrected him, saying that her name was Marie. He walked away and she continued on her way out. Back in the school, Wolverine, Storm, Beast, Iceman, and Kitty gathered to decide about the school's future. Hank thought that Professor Xavier started this school and that it was best that it end with him. He told Bobby that they would have to tell all the students they would have to go home, but Bobby said that most of them have no home to go to. He thought that they should fight for this school. Before anyone could respond, a young man walked in. It's Warren Worthington III, the mutant [[Angel]]. He apologized for coming at a bad time, but he was told that this is a safe place for mutants. Beast sadly told him it was, but Ororo corrected him and told Angel that it still was. She told Warren they would find him a room and told Hank to tell all the students that the school would stay open, much to Iceman's delight. Outside, Logan walked to Xavier's memorial. Suddenly, he heard Jean's voice whispering his name in his head repeatedly. He jumped and turned around to see where it was coming from but it just kept getting louder and louder. He called her name and visions of the forest popped into his mind along with her voice. Logan then fell to his knees, holding his stomach in pain as Jean's voice continued to echo in his mind. Then, it stopped, and he looked up at Xavier's face sculptured in the memorial. Immediately, he left to prepare to take off. Storm found him and asked where he was off too, telling him that Jean was gone – she wasn't coming back. Logan said that Storm didn't know that, but Ororo reminded him that Jean was the one who killed the professor. Wolverine defended her, saying that it wasn't Jean – the Jean he knew was still in there. Storm asked him why he couldn't see the truth and let Jean go, and when he didn't answer, she says it was because he loves her. She told him Jean made her choice and now it was time they make theirs. "If you are with us," she said, "then you should be with us." Having finished what she had to say, Storm left the room. Wolverine chooses not to listen, though, and left the mansion on his motorcycle. [[File:Wolverine301.jpg|left|thumb|250px|Wolverine battles against [[Magneto]]'s henchmen.]] Wolverine arrived to the forest where the Brotherhood was camped with the help of his hyper senses. He sniffed around to find the exact location. When he locked on a specific direction, he popped his claws and starts running but is stopped a few feet later by two large spikes shot at his shoulders. He fell back, wounded, but quickly got back up to his feet to see his attacker – a Brotherhood member called [[Spike]], who seemed ready to attack again. Logan started running away, dodging the many spikes shot at him at dazzling speed. Not far away, another Brotherhood member with increased hearing heard the spikes shooting and realized something was up. Along with a few more mutants, he headed to help Spike. Spike kept running after Logan and shooting him, not letting go of his opponent. Logan hid behind a tree to rest for a second and used his claws to get the spike out of his shoulder. He waited for Spike to make another move, then jumped out of his hiding, sending one of Spike's spikes flying back at him with the help of his claws. Spike was hit and fell down, but Logan knew that wasn't the end of it, sensing more mutants coming. He hurried to climb the tree and wait, then jumped down and surprised the Brotherhood mutants. Before they could realize what was going on, he took them down one by one in a berserk rage, slashing them with his claws. Spike got up and threw another spike at Wolverine, but Logan blocked it with his claws and started running towards Spike, blocking more and more spikes as he ran. As they got closer to each other, they jumped at one another and both hit their target. Wolverine got up with two spikes stuck in his stomach. Still, he was victorious, as Spike was in a much worse shape, having been stabbed by his claws. Logan sheathes his claws and took out the spikes, then continued to the Brotherhood's camp. In the camp, Magneto was giving a speech to the others. Logan arrived in the middle of it and took a cloak from one of the tents to cover himself. Magneto told his followers that the humans wished to cure them and he says to them: the mutants were the cure. Everyone cheered and Magneto went on, saying that they were the cure for the imperfect condition called Homo Sapiens. "Humans have their weapons and mutants have theirs," he said as he looked at Phoenix. They planned to strike with a vengeance and fury that this world had never witnessed and, if any mutants stood in their way, they would use the cure against them. Logan stood in the crowd and listened as Magneto detailed his plan: they would go to Alcatraz Island, take control of the cure and destroy its source, and then nothing could stop them. Phoenix, standing behind Magneto, spotted Wolverine in the crowd and he noticed that she saw him. She walked aside and he did the same, getting away from the camp. He saw her walking away and called her name. She looked at him but doesn't have time to say a word as Magneto arrived and tossed Logan into a large tree, injuring him. Magneto told Logan that he could sense the smell of adamantium a mile away. Logan said that he didn't come to fight Magneto – he came for Jean. Erik asked if Wolverine thought he was keeping Jean against her will, and when Logan didn't answer, Erik told him that Jean/Phoenix was there because she wanted to be. He held Wolverine in the air with his powers and Logan told him he didn't know what he was dealing with. Magneto answered that he knew full well – he saw what she had done to Charles. Wolverine said that he wasn't leaving without Jean, but Magneto thought otherwise and used his powers to send Logan flying far away. Wolverine eventually hit a tall tree and fell to the ground, beaten and injured. Back in the school, Wolverine ran in after his encounter with Magneto and called for Storm hysterically. He found her, and she asked him what he was doing back there. He told her that he needed help – he had found Jean with Magneto and they were on the move, and he knew where they were going. Beast joined them, asking Logan if he really saw Magneto. Logan told them he had, and they had to go now, before the Brotherhood attacked Alcatraz. Hank reminded him that the government had troops stationed out there, but Wolverine didn't think it was enough to stop Magneto. As they walked hurriedly to get ready, Angel overheard them and realized his [[Warren Worthington II|father]], who found the mutant 'cure', was in danger and needed help. A few minutes later, Beast and Wolverine were ready in uniform in the mansion's tunnels. Logan told Hank that if Magneto got ahold of the cure, he would be unstoppable. Hank asked how many mutants Magneto had on his side and Logan says that he has an army. They meet up with Storm, Iceman, Kitty and Colossus, and Bobby reminded Logan that there were only six of them. Wolverine paused and told the young students that he wouldn't lie to them – they were outnumbered, they lost Scott, they lost the professor – but if they didn't fight now, everything those men stood for would die with them, and he was not going to let that happen. Iceman nodded in agreement and Logan said that they stand together, X-Men, all of them. Bobby looked at Kitty and Peter, and they both signaled they were ready. He then turned back to Logan and told him they were in. Ready for the battle of their lives, the X-Men walked towards the Blackbird. On the way, Wolverine told Storm the kids were ready. She agreed, but asked him if he was ready to do what he would have to do when the time comes. Not knowing what to answer, he boarded the Blackbird and they take off, heading to San Francisco. In the back, the three kids sat in silence, scared – but ready. The Blackbird approached the battle scene at high speed and the X-Men are amazed to see the Golden Gate Bridge bridge and what was happening – it was worse than they had ever dreamed. Callisto sensed something was coming. It was the X-Men's Blackbird landing on the roof. Lightning fell on the battle scene as Storm descended from the sky. Next, Beast jumped from the roof and landed like a true animal. Iceman and Kitty jumped together, phasing into the ground to avoid getting squashed and then phasing right back up. Colossus jumped down too, his metal form protecting him. Lastly, Wolverine jumped down the wall, using his claws to scratch the wall and slow down his fall. Storm landed too, and Wolverine yelled to the humans to go back inside the building and lock the doors. He then ordered the X-Men to stand together and hold a line. Instantly, the six mutants lined up in front of the Brotherhood to form a strict line of defense, ready for the battle of their lives. [[File:Wolverinebattle.jpg|thumb|250px|Wolverine at Alcatraz.]] On the hill, Magneto looked at the X-Men, whispering to himself that they were traitors to their own cause. Enraged, he then ordered the Brotherhood to finish them. Magneto's army immediately charged forward at the X-Men. Wolverine slashed a light pole with his claws and it dropped on the charging Brotherhood, killing some of them. The battle then begun, with Wolverine and Beast succeeding in taking down Brotherhood members rather quickly with their furious animal attacks. Up on the hill, Magneto told Juggernaut to go inside and find the boy – and kill him. Juggernaut said that he would do it with pleasure and ran towards the building, not letting anything stand in his way. Wolverine sees him run straight through the wall and Beast realized he was going for the boy. "Not if I get there first", Kitty replied, and ran in the same direction, phasing through anything in her path. A mutant named [[Starfish]], who has the ability to grow back his organs, attacked Wolverine. Logan tried to slash him like he did with all other mutants, but it was no use as the mutant quickly grew back his slashed arms every time and would hit Wolverine when he wasn't ready. Having had enough, Logan kicked the mutant is the groin and he finally fell down. "Grow those back," Logan said, smirking, as he moved on to his next opponent. Beast and Wolverine both went into a berserk rage and took down Brotherhood members in large numbers. Logan told Beast that he thought Hank was a diplomat. Hank explained, saying, "There comes a time when all men must-" He stops mid-sentence to beat up some more mutants and just yells to Logan "Oh, you get the point!". Up on the hill, Magneto told Pyro that it was time to end this war. He used his powers to lift a car from the bridge behind them and threw it at the battle scene, with Pyro torching it along the way. The flaming car landed and exploded and everyone ran away. Wolverine yelled to the X-Men to take cover as Magneto continued on to the second attempt with another torched car. He kept on launching more and more cars, each time Pyro torching the cars halfway to make them explode. Storm found cover behind a column but was almost hit. She continued on to a better cover along with Wolverine and the others. Near them, they see one of the cure guns. Looking at the apocalyptic view around them – the place in ruins, cars exploding all around them – the X-Men had to make a decision. Wolverine reminded Storm of what she had told him in the Danger Room session earlier – they work as a team. She, in turn, reminded him of what he had answered – the best defense was a good offense. Logan turned to Bobby and asked him if he thought he could take down his old friend Pyro. Bobby nodded and ran out of the cover, using his ice-powers to freeze one of the flying cars in the air, and turned off the flames before it could explode. Wolverine told Storm that he would need some cover for his next move and she understood perfectly, using her powers to create fog. Magneto and Phoenix, the only ones left on the hill, looked around to find the rest of the X-Men, but the fog kept them from finding them. Wolverine spotted Magneto through the fog and pointed him out to Colossus. Just like in the Danger Room, Colossus grabbed Wolverine and spun him around, tossing him at Magneto. It failed, though, as Magneto saw Wolverine and used his powers to stop Logan. Logan fell to the ground and slid on his stomach, stopping right before Magneto's legs. Erik then flipped Logan on his back, telling him that he never learned. "Actually," Wolverine answered, "I do." Just then, Beast jumped at Magneto from behind and injected him with the cure they took from the gun. Magneto dropped to his knees, realizing he was now human, and fell back. Rolling on the ground, he told Jean in a shaking voice that this was what the other humans and the X-Men wanted for them. "It's over, Jean." Logan said to his love, the last Brotherhood member left standing. The moment was broken, though, when the soldiers used the silence to shoot at her with the cure guns. Logan yelled at them not to shoot, but it was too late. Jean stopped the shots telekinetically and turned around – the fight wasn't over. Wolverine cried "No!" as Phoenix levitated into the air and blew up all the light poles. Again, a telekinetic storm started to rage. Beast yelled for everybody to run out of the building and escape, and they all fled in panic. Kitty and Leech got out of the building and found Iceman, and together they ran away. Staring at Phoenix's madness, Erik mumbled, "What have I done?" Frightened, he then joined the others fleeing to the bridge. Storm ran up to Wolverine, and he told her he was the only one who could stop Phoenix. He ordered her to get everyone to safety and leave, and she flew to the sky. He cried out to Jean but she doesn't respond. Seeing the destruction around him, Logan called Jean's name again, saying he knew she was still somewhere inside of Phoenix's body. He started walking towards Phoenix but she held him back with her powers. Still, he struggled, walking slowly against her power. Only a few feet were left between then – Phoenix stood on the top of the hill, and Wolverine a little below. He struggled to climb, sticking his claws to the ground to move. Eventually, he managed to get to her and stood in front of her. Amazed that he had fought his way through and got to her, Phoenix opened her mouth for the first time since she lay foot on Alcatraz Island, asking Logan if he'd really die for them. "No," he answered. "Not for them. For you." Hearing this, the Jean Grey part of her personality rose back for a moment, and she begged to him once again in tears to save her. He told her he loved her, and then does what he knew he had to – what she had asked him to do before in the infirmary – he popped his claws in her gut, killing the woman he loved. In her dying breath, Jean looked at him in silence and smiled – it was over. Her body fell to his arms, and he cries. The next day, in another room, Logan watched the president on TV giving a speech in the White House: "So it is with the thanks of a grateful nation, that I introduce our new ambassador to the United Nations and a representative to the world for all United States citizens – human and mutant alike: Dr. Hank McCoy." The president then shook Hank's hand. "Way to go, furball," Logan thought to himself. He then walked out and looked at the school that he and Storm were now left in charge of. ===Reunion with Ichirō Yashida=== {{Quote|Look at me. A lot of people have tried to kill me, and I'm still here.|Wolverine to [[Yukio]]|The Wolverine}} Having a flashback, Logan remembered how he had saved Ichiro Yashida, the Japanese soldier, from the nuclear bombing in Nagasaki. He heard Jean's voice in his head and seemed to wake up from his nightmare. She asked him where he had been, referring to his nightmare, and, still panting from his dream, he told her, "Nagasaki." In disbelief, he sat up and as Jean gently kissed his shoulders to comfort him, he said, mostly to himself, "You're here." "Of course." she said and Logan asked if she could stay while touching her cheek. She responded, saying that he knew she couldn't, and he started apologizing for killing her and revealed that he had made a vow to never hurt anyone ever again. Jean whispered in his ear that it was too late, looking down, and he followed her gaze to see his claws unsheathed in her stomach with blood spilling out. He started screaming, repeating, "No, Jean!" while she told him that he couldn't hide. Gasping, he actually awoke from the nightmare back to reality in 2013. He was lying down in the cave in the side of a mountain in the Canadian wilderness where he lived, isolated, having left the X-Men over the guilt of Jean's death. The only possessions he had were photos, including one of Jean, a radio, and a few other trinkets. He had also regained the memories he lost in 1979. He trekked through the forest to the nearby town for necessary supplies. While walking, he came across a bear who was headed in the same direction he was. They acknowledged each other and the bear did not attack him. Logan walked through town into a store to buy supplies and spotted a group of men buying hunting materials. [[File:Logan03-TW.png|left|thumb|250px|Wolverine in the Canadian wilderness.]] Later that night, in his cave, Logan heard a gunshot from the woods and a man's scream. Going to investigate, he found a camp set up in the forest and a bear's paw print in the ground near the camp. Walking around the campsite, he finds the bear that he had seen earlier that day lying on the floor, wounded and dying slowly with an arrow in its side. Deciding to end its pain for it, he stabbed the bear with his claws, killing it swiftly. He walked to the town and into a bar where one of the men he had seen in the store with hunting weapons was telling a story about how a bear had killed the other men that were there with him, phrasing the story as if the bear had attacked ruthlessly and he had been lucky to survive. Walking towards the man, Logan pretended to want to buy him a drink but, when he got close enough, stabbed him through the hand with the same arrow he had found in the bear. He pointed out that the arrow had been poisoned, which was illegal. One of the other men tried to attack him, but Logan easily knocked him back. He then berated the man for letting the bear die slowly and painfully and for using the illegal poisoned arrow, which had made the bear go crazy and attack the others. The man broke a bottle over Logan's head and Logan popped his claws, ready to fight, but, suddenly, a Japanese girl, [[Yukio]], who had been following him, stopped him, saying they would die soon anyway. The men prepared to attack them but Yukio demonstrated her ability to fight with a sword and successfully intimidated the men so that they wouldn't follow them. She told Logan to follow her to her car, which was parked behind the bar. Logan got into her car and, while she was driving, asked who she was. Yukio introduced herself, saying that she worked for 'Master Yashida', the Japanese soldier Logan had saved during Nagasaki. She had been tasked with giving him the sword Yashida had promised to give Logan and to bring him to Japan to meet Yashida so that Yashida could thank him in person, since he was dying. At first, Logan refused, saying that he wasn't going all the way to Japan to say goodbye to someone and that he was a different person now. After Yukio insisted that he was, saying that he was a soldier and she knew he was in pain, which was evident from his recurring nightmares, and seeing police cars drive past them to the bar, Logan agreed to go to Japan but only for one day. On the plane, Logan's fear of flying once again presented itself. Yukio revealed that she was a mutant who could see into the future. Once in Japan, as they were driving to Yashida's residency, Logan sees billboards everywhere with the symbol of [[Yashida Corporation]]. Yukio told Logan that there had been many attempts on Yashida's life recently by the [[Yakuza]]. While waiting to meet Yashida, Logan saw [[Shingen Yashida|Shingen]], Yashida's son, training, and commented on how impressive his fighting was. Through a slightly opening in the door to the medical room where Yashida was lying, Logan and Yukio see a young woman holding Yashida's hand and talking frantically to him. Yukio told Logan that that woman was [[Mariko Yashida]], Yashida's granddaughter. Crying, she exited the room, walking past Logan and Yukio but Yukio followed her, trying to find out what was wrong. Glancing at Logan, Mariko didn't tell Yukio anything. After he got cleaned up, Logan walked into the room where Yashida was and passed a female blonde doctor, then met Yashida. Looking around the room, Logan saw two bottles filled with a blue liquid with a strange bug inside each bottle. He glanced at the live X-rays of Yashida's heart and saw two of the same bugs crawling around in his chest. Then, Yashida offered to repay Logan for saving his life with something bizarre — an offer to take his immortality away from him and give it instead to Yashida himself, who didn't want to die. Shocked, Logan refused the offer. As he walked out, Yashida said to him to that Mariko needed to be protected. Logan and Yukio then saw Shingen slap Mariko and Mariko try to jump off a nearby cliff. Logan grabbed her just in time. Later, Yukio told Logan that Yashida found her on a past trip and had taken her in to be Mariko's friend and protector. That night, Logan dreamed about Jean again and they started kissing, but it was revealed to be Viper, the blonde female doctor he had seen in Yashida's room, who was really kissing him, and she injected something into his body through his mouth. After waking up in a fright, he heard people shouting and Yukio came into his room to inform him of Yashida's death, and that she hadn't foreseen it. [[File:Wolverine (Japan).jpg|right|thumb|250px|Yashida's funeral brings Yakuza assassins.]] The next morning, Logan attended Yashida's funeral, where he sees Viper, [[Kenuichio Harada]], who has dedicated his life to protecting Mariko, watching them from the top of the building, and [[Noburo Mori|Noburo]], Mariko's fiance. He had a short conversation with Noburo where Noburo told him to go back to Canada. Later on in the funeral, while people were paying their respects, he noticed strange tattoos on the funeral staff escorting Mariko. He ran to her, interrupting the funeral and grabbed her arm, insisting that something wasn't right. However, she hasn't realized this yet and tells him to let her go. He does, but doesn't back off as she walks away. Still trying to figure out what was wrong, Logan approaches another staff member slowly while someone shouted at him to back away, and when he got too close, the staff took out a shotgun from under his robes and shot Logan, creating chaos. Something was wrong as Logan wasn't able to recover from the shot as quickly as he used to. In a dazed state, he watches Mariko being snatched away before snapping out off it and following her. He slashed through the Yakuza members to get to her as Yukio tried to follow from behind, but was eventually stopped and separated from them by Shingen. While he was fighting, Viper filmed him and, when confronted by an assassin, spat venom on the assassin's face and killed him. With every shot, Logan becomes more injured and weak, rendering him unable to fight at his full power. As he made his way through to Mariko, Harada helped him by shooting down people who were following him with arrows from the surrounding rooftops. Having reached Mariko, they ran through the city and across a busy road, with the cars taking out some of their followers. Mariko fought off a couple of Yakuza members, displaying her fighting ability. Harada continued to follow and defend them and, as Logan and Mariko run through different stores, he was eventually able to take down all of their attackers with arrows. While they ran, Mariko commented that Logan was bleeding and needed help. She thanked him for helping her but, having reached a train station with all their attackers gone, Mariko insisted that she could make her own way from there. Doubtful, Logan followed her onto a bullet train with more Yakuza members behind him. Meanwhile, Noburo talked to reporters at the funeral and said that this Yakuza attack was a retaliation to his efforts to track down organized crime. Shingen was told that Mariko had escaped with Logan and took Yukio aside to forcefully ask her where Logan was, and then she responded, "Protecting your daughter," he told her that she was nothing more than a toy doll, and with Mariko grown up, their family had no more need for her. Back on the train, Logan asked Mariko where they were going, and Mariko tells him that at the end of the line, their family had a home in the South where no one would look for her. Logan told her that that wasn't going to work and they would find her, but Mariko ignored him. He checked his gunshot wounds, which weren't healing, and headed to the bathroom to fix himself up. While in the bathroom, he ran into more Yakuza members and started fighting them. He got shot numerous times and they ended up on the roof of the train. With one more assassin to kill, they faced each other on the top of the train. Logan, who was facing the front, saw that there was a sign coming and feinted a jump, convincing the assassin that there was a lower sign heading towards them and made the assassin jump, which led to him being knocked out by the higher sign. Looking back, Logan saw one more assassin who was looking through the sunroof at Mariko sitting in her seat. Having defeated the killers, Logan ran into the train and told Mariko that they were getting off as the train stopped at a station. He told Mariko that they needed to get someplace safe and off the street but Mariko kept asking him how he knew they were after her and she kept saying that she didn't understand. She guided him through the town and they ended up at a hotel that was eight blocks away from the "nice part of town." Inside, Mariko told him that this was a love hotel, but he didn't care. She talked with the hotel staff and got them a space-themed room. Logan kept watch over their room in the rain as she slept, but he hallucinated Jean and, bleeding out from his wounds, passed out, alerting Mariko. [[File:Logan08-TW.png|left|thumb|250px|Wolverine on the bullet train.]] He awakened to find that Mariko had gotten the motel owner's grandson, a veterinarian, attending to his wounds. He reached out his hand to thank him, but the grandson backed away and Mariko told him that, while unconscious, he had cut him a few times. He asked her who the blonde doctor, Viper, was, and she told him that Yashida had met her when he went to America the previous year for treatment. Logan told her that Viper had taken his healing from him. Meanwhile, Viper walked through a busy alleyway and, when bothered by a Japanese business man who thought she was a prostitute, killed him. She went on her way to meet Harada and he berated her for keeping him waiting for two hours, calling her disrespectful and telling her that he could kill her if he wanted. Viper said that he could not and blew poison into his mouth, causing welts on his face. It was her turn to threaten him, scolding him for not being able to bring her Logan when she had done her part and weakened him. She blew into his face again, healing him, and told him it was her he should respect and to find Logan and Mariko before the Yakuza did. The next day, Logan and Mariko got off a bus at the place near where Logan and saved Yashida all those years ago. That evening, Mariko told Logan that Harada used to be her boyfriend and that they had been village champions at one point, him with the bow and her with knives. They had wanted to get married, but Yashida stopped them since they hadn't been even 15 years old. Mariko and Logan then ate their dinner, and Logan stuck his chopsticks upright in his food but Mariko took them out and placed them next to his bowl, telling him that chopsticks upright resembled incense at a funeral and were a bad omen. He questioned her about why she had tried to kill herself that night, why Shingen had slapped her, and what Yashida had told her that night. Hesitantly, she told him that it was because Yashida had written in his will to hand over the company to her instead of her father, Shingen. She said that she didn't understand why Yashida did it and Logan said that that was precisely why he had done it. He asked Mariko why she was engaged to Noburo since he seemed like "an asshole" and she told him that it was an arranged marriage by her father, because it gave Shingen "better access to the political theatre." She said that to disobey her father would be to dishonor him and she didn't expect Logan to understand, and then reached over to, again, put his chopsticks that he had stuck upright in his food back beside his bowl. Back in Yashida's house, Shingen berates the police force for not being able to find Mariko. He talks with Noburo, who says that he was sending his men to the South to look for Mariko, since they found bodies of the Yakuza there. Logan tried shaving but ended up cutting himself and he touched the cut tenderly, realizing, as the cut did not quickly heal, that he was mortal. Meanwhile, Yukio and Mariko talked on the phone and Yukio said that the Yakuza must have had someone on the inside. Yukio told Mariko that she was coming to them, but Mariko pleaded with her to give them a few days since Yukio would be followed. There was a knock on the door and an old woman told Mariko that a tree had fallen on the road and asked for Logan's help. After helping the men, Logan sat down, tired. While resting, he saw the place where he had saved Yashida and had a flashback to when Yashida had given him the sword in thanks. He had held the sword in his right hand and Yashida had told him to use both hands. They then climbed out of the hole they had hidden in to survive the blast and Yashida started crying, seeing the damage to his country. Mariko said that Yashida had told her stories of him and that everything in the world would find peace eventually. She commented on how he didn't sleep well and called out in his sleep, and asked who Jean was. He avoided the question, looking out at the sea and Mariko said that they should head back as rain was coming. They made it back to the house just as it started storming and after Logan had changed into a traditional Japanese yukata, Mariko told him that he had tied it wrongly and helped him fix it. While doing so, he told her that Yashida had called him a ronin, a samurai without a master, and that he was destined to live that way. She asked if he had been right, and when Logan said yes, she asked again if he was still right, looking up and him and hinting at a romance blossoming between them. After a moment of silence, he leaned in and kissed her and they started making out. That night, as they lie in bed together, Logan hallucinates an image of Jean saying that everyone he loved would die and then an image of the samurai armour that had been stationed by Yashida's casket attacking him. He popped his claws, worked up, and Mariko touched his fist gently to comfort him. She told him that Kuzuri, which was a name many people had called Logan while he was in Japan, was a dangerous animal who feared nothing. Yashida had told her when she was young that Kuzuri would protect her, and asked again about Jean. Logan finally opened up and told her that Jean was dead and he had killed her along with the Kuzuri. Yukio awoke in the middle of the night from a vision she had about Logan's death. The next morning, Logan woke to the sound of Mariko screaming and being taken away by men. He ran after her but didn't get to her in time; however, he was able to defeat one of the men and found out that Noburo had hired them. Yukio picked him up in her car and took him to where Noburo was staying. She told him that she had a vision of him dying with his heart, not beating, in his hand. She adds that when she was five, she saw how her own parents would die and watched it happen. He tells her that people had tried to kill him before and hadn't succeeded, but she tells him that he was different now that he couldn't heal. He told her to wait in the car while he talked to Noburo but she followed him out, saying that she would be his bodyguard since he had lost his healing ability. They walk into his apartment to find Noburo with two strippers, and Logan tells them to get out. After refusing to tell him why he had hired men to kidnap Mariko. He revealed that Shingen had promised him a large sum of money to kidnap her, since they both knew that Yashida planned to hand the company over to Mariko. Enraged, Logan thew him out of his apartment into the pool below. The men had brought Mariko to meet Shingen, who was about to kill her when a bunch of people hired by Harada ambushed him and took Mariko away. Viper walked into Shingen's room and stabbed him with a poisoned pen and he ran out into the nearby pond. Yukio and Logan followed close behind to find Yashida's place empty, with a note with a picture of Yashida's birthplace that said to find her there. In the room that Yashida had been receiving treatment in, Logan once again saw the two bottoles filled with blue liquid, but this time empty of the bugs. He lay on the bed and activated the machine that scanned his chest to find a bug on his heart, which was presumably stopping his healing. He sliced his own chest open, trying to get the parasite out, but Yukio begged him not to, scared that her vision of him dying with his heart in his hands would come true. He went ahead anyway, reaching into his chest. While doing so, Shingen walked into the room with a sword and a samurai set of armour on and walked towards Logan. [[File:Thewolverine6.jpg|right|thumb|250px|Wolverine prepares to fight against Shingen Yashida.]] Since Wolverine was in a vulnerable position, Yukio was left to defend him. Logan gets the parasite out but flatlines, but, having had his healing ability restored, returned to life and started fighting Shingen, who was about to kill Yukio. Shingen called mutants "God's mistakes" and cut Logan with a samurai sword, but was horrified when Logan quickly healed. Logan walked away from Shingen, telling him that he had to live with the fact that he tried to kill his daughter. However, Shingen then ran at Logan who then turned around and killed him. Mariko asked Harada why he had brought her to the facility that Yashida Corporation was building at his birthplace, and Viper walked in. Scared, Mariko took a step back but Harada told her to relax since she also worked for the House of Yashida. Viper then stabbed Mariko in the neck with her fingernail that contained poison, causing her to pass out. After waking up, Mariko walked towards Viper and told Viper that she was now the head of the family, which meant that Viper should take commands from her. However, Viper spins the ledge they are standing around to face a machine that looked like a samurai, asking Mariko why she thought Yashida had chosen her to take over the corporation — because she was strong, or weak? Logan and Yukio arrived at the Yashida Research Center in northern Japan and Logan went in to find Mariko. He was stopped by Harada, who thanked him for his protection of Mariko but said that he had to stop there. [[Black Clan|The Black Clan]], who had protected the House of Yashida for 700 years, then took down Logan with poisoned arrows. [[File:Wol5.jpg|left|thumb|250px|Wolverine placed in a machine by Viper along with Silver Samurai.]] Logan woke up trapped in a machine created by Viper. She walked in and he asked her angrily where Mariko was, but she ignored him and showed him the samurai machine, telling him that it was made of adamantium, just like him. Yukio broke into the facility. Viper continued taunting him and he eventually popped his claws, triggering the part of the machine around his wrist to move, preventing him from retracing his adamantium claws. Mariko watched him from across the building and Harada told her to let go of her attachment to him. However, when Harada kissed her and his guard was lowered, she stabbed him with his own knife, injuring but not killing him, and ran to Logan, who was about to get his claws cut off by the [[Silver Samurai (armor)|Silver Samurai]]. As it swung its sword, Mariko ran in front of it, causing it to miss its target and break the machine that was holding Logan in place instead. As Logan and Mariko ran away, Viper chased them but was shot down by Harada, who had come to his senses. She fell down the building but didn't die. Mariko told Logan to run but he tackled the Silver Samurai instead. He used his claws to fend it off but the Samurai used its red-hot adamantium sword to cut off his claws. This caused Logan to scream in pain as his bone had also been cut away. Left in a vulnerable position, the Samurai kicked him farther down the building. As he got up on the ledge that he lands on, he saw Viper's skin on the floor, which she had molted out of. Looking up, he sees her and she spat in his face with poison. Rendered blind for a few moments, Logan was unable to see the Samurai land near him and come at him with its sword. However, he used his remaining claws to slash the power pox of the building, turning off the lights so that he would be able to hide in the darkness while he healed from the Viper's poison. He then attacked the Samurai. As Logan fought the Samurai, Yukio fought Viper. Logan was eventually defeated and as Logan was about to have his remaining claws slashed off by the Samurai, Harada shot it in its face, making it drop one of its red-hot sword, and some blood dripped out of the machine. Harada ran to the machine and, holding the arrow still stuck in its face, told it, "This is not the way." However, whoever was inside the machine did not listen and instead stabbed him. Meanwhile, Logan found the sword that the Samurai dropped but was unable to activate it. Remembering what Yashida had told him in the pit, he used two hands to grab it and it started heating up. Harada begged the Samurai not to kill him, calling it "Master," but is then flung away. Logan then cuts off its head using its own sword and kicked it down the building. He jumped down with it but fell off a ledge, barely hanging on to the side of the building. Mariko ran to Harada, who was dying, and apologized for stabbing him earlier. He then died. As Logan tried to climb his way back to safety, the Samurai came back to life and cut off his remaining claws. He started to fall, but the Samurai grabbed his arm and started to drill into his claws, harvesting his healing ability. As it raised him by his arms to face it, Logan looked inside the armour and saw the human inside it — Yashida. Yashida told Logan that the armour was the only thing keeping him alive as he began to get younger, with Logan becoming weaker. Just as Logan was about to die, Mariko, holding Logan's claws that had been cut off, walked behind Yashida and threw one of the claws, stabbing him in the head. She called him a monster and he tried to tell her that he was her grandfather, but she simply said that she had buried her grandfather and stabbed him again, killing him. Yukio stood behind Mariko and watched as Logan tore apart the armour keeping Yashida alive, then used his bone claws, which had grown back, to stab him and throw him off the side of the building as he said goodbye to Yashida. Logan then collapsed and had another hallucination of Jean telling him to stay with her this time, but he refused and finally healed from his trauma of her. She walked away and he told her one last time that he loved her, that he always would. Later, Logan said goodbye to Mariko, who was now CEO of the Yashida Corporation as she asked him to stay. He refused, saying that he was a soldier and had been away for too long. He boarded a plane with Yukio, who reminded him that she was still his bodyguard. Yukio then said to Wolverine that they could go wherever they wanted to and they depart to somewhere unknown. Two years later, in 2015, Logan is at an airport and is approached by Magneto and Charles Xavier about a new threat to wipe out all mutants. In some point between 2015 and 2023, Logan's bone claws were once again coated in adamantium, presumably with Magneto's help. ===Hunted by Sentinels=== [[File:Wolverine-DOFP.jpg|right|thumb|250px|Wolverine in the dystopian future.]] {{Quote|What if someone's mind has a way of snapping back? What someone can heal as fast as they're being torn apart?|Wolverine|X-Men: Days of Future Past}} Eight years later, the surviving X-men, called the [[Free Mutants]], are living in an apocalyptic world where all mutants were being hunted down and killed, and any humans that tried to help them were also executed. This world was made possible by machines built by the government called [[Sentinels]]. The key point in gaining governmental support for the Sentinel program was when Mystique had killed [[Bolivar Trask]], the person who had proposed the idea of Sentinels, as that event had shown the government the need for Sentinels. The Free Mutants have been surviving by hiding out in various bases and when discovered by Sentinels, [[Kitty Pryde|Shadowcat]], a mutant who can travel back through time, goes back a few days to warn their past selves about the coming attack. Wolverine, Storm, Professor X, and Magneto arrive to find their fellow Free Mutants in [[China]], where they planned to send Wolverine back to 1973 to stop Mystique from killing Bolivar Trask in hopes of preventing Sentinels from ever being created. Because of his healing abilities, he was the only one who could physically and mentally survive the trip back to the past. Shadowcat sent his mind to the past, back to his younger body, while his physical body remained in the present. ===Days of Future Past=== ====Changing the Past==== {{Quote|In the beginning, the Sentinels were just targeting mutants. Then they began to identify the genetics in non-mutants who'd eventually have mutant children or grandchildren, and they started targeting everyone. Many of the humans tried to help us, it was a slaughter... leaving only the worst of humanity in charge. I've been in a lot of wars, I've never seen anything like this. And it all starts with her.|Wolverine|X-Men: Days of Future Past}} [[File:Xmen-days-future-movie-screencaps.com-3402.jpg|left|thumb|250x250px|Wolverine visits Beast in the X-Mansion.]] In 1973, Wolverine woke up in 1973 in bed next to a woman whom he had been hired to protect, and three men who worked for the woman's father entered and shot at him. Wolverine, confused, killed them and went to find Professor X. Wolverine headed to the X-Mansion where he makes a rough encounter with [[Beast/Revised Timeline|Hank McCoy/Beast]].. Wolverine saw [[Professor X/Revised Timeline|Professor X]] and asked for his help in preventing Bolivar Trask's death, but this was not the same Charles that he knew. Charles wasn't in a wheelchair, was able to walk, but was also unable to use his powers. He had become a desolate alcoholic who had had his school closed because of the Vietnam War. He had lost his hope for mutants but after realizing that he could have a chance of seeing Mystique, who had joined Magneto, again after all these years, he reluctantly agreed to help Wolverine. They planned to break Magneto out of prison, who was there for killing President [[John F. Kennedy]]. The three first headed to [[Quicksilver|Peter Maximoff/Quicksilver]]'s house and asked him for help. At the Pentagon, Charles, Wolverine, Quicksilver, and Hank snuck into the facility and freed Erik. [[File:Xmen-days-future-movie-screencaps.com-10042.jpg|right|thumb|250px|Professor X reads Wolverine's mind.]] While pursuing and capturing Mystique, Wolverine found William Stryker and had traumatic flashbacks. In 2023, Wolverine started violently shaking and accidentally slashed Shadowcat, who was continuously using her power on him to keep his mind in the past, in the rib with his claws. Back in 1973, he briefly forgot why he was there and who young Charles is. Later, when a depressed Charles is unable to use [[Cerebro]] to find Mystique, Logan has him use his mind as a "bridge" to his future self, allowing the two Charles Xavier's to communicate through him. The future Charles Xavier convinces his past self not to give up and he is able to use Cerebro once more. [[File:Xmen-days-future-movie-screencaps.com-13197.jpg|left|thumb|250px|The trio search for [[Mystique|Raven Darkholme]] in Washington, D.C.]] As Wolverine, Professor X and Hank go to the unveiling of the Sentinels to find Mystique, the Sentinels begin attacking the public. Magneto lifts an entire stadium and transports it to surround the White House and puts all but one Sentinel on lookout and makes it go after Wolverine and Beast. Wolverine attacks it but Magneto stabs him with reinforcement steel then drowns him in a nearby river. While Wolverine is incapacitated, his actions lead to the past Charles Xavier finishing what he started and changing the future. Back in 2023, the Sentinels are close to firing upon the X-Men but everyone disappears after Shadowcat finally breaks the connection, causing Wolverine's changes to the timeline to take effect. Having successfully altered the timeline, Wolverine awakens in the Xavier Institute in the new future at precisely the same time he was sent back in time, to find [[Storm/Revised Timeline|Storm]], [[Shadowcat/Revised Timeline|Shadowcat]], [[Rogue/Revised Timeline|Rogue]], [[Iceman/Revised Timeline|Iceman]], [[Colossus/Revised Timeline|Colossus]], [[Beast/Revised Timeline|Beast]], and to his shock, [[Cyclops/Revised Timeline|Cyclops]] and [[Jean Grey/Revised Timeline|Jean Grey]] alive again. Wolverine visits Professor X and reveals he has just finished time traveling and asks what happened in the last fifty years, apparently possessing no memory of the events of the new timeline. A delighted Professor X tells him they have a lot to catch up on. ===History Rewritten=== ====Joining the X-Men==== {{Quote|It's good to see you Charles. It's good to see everyone.|Wolverine|X-Men: Days of Future Past}} [[File:Xmen-days-future-movie-screencaps.com-16344.jpg|left|thumb|250px|Logan in 2023.]] In 2023, the consciousness of Logan from the original timeline woke up in his body in this timeline. Upon leaving his room, he saw how friends that had been killed in the original timeline - [[Iceman]], [[Rogue]], [[Beast]], [[Storm]], [[Kitty Pryde]], [[Colossus]], [[Cyclops]] and [[Jean Grey]] - at the school, a sign they had succeeded and the timeline had been corrected. He walked into Professor X’s office, briefly talking to him about what happened, and told him that he needed to get caught up on the last fifty years. ====Westchester Incident==== In 2028, Charles Xavier would develop Alzheimer's and suffer a massive seizure that injured six hundred people and killed several members of the X-Men. Logan would take Charles to a place in Mexico near the U.S border. He would also ask for the assistance of [[Caliban]] to help care for Charles. ====Finding Something to Die For==== [[File:Logan(Film)Still3.jpg|thumb|250px|Logan in 2029]] {{Quote|Charles, the world is not the same as it was.|Logan to Charles Xavier|Logan (film)}} In 2029, Logan is working as a Limo driver and has started going by his birth name James Howlett where he escorts all types of people from businessmen to high schoolers, hoping to save up enough money that he can buy a boat and take Charles, himself, and [[Caliban]] out to sea where they will not have to worry about Charles' seizures hurting anyone. One day, Logan is approached by [[Gabriela Lopez|Gabriela]], a nurse for Transigen, who asks him to escort her and an 11-year-old girl, [[Laura]], to a place in North Dakota called [[Eden]]. Logan is later met by [[Donald Pierce]], Transigen's cybernetically-enhanced chief of security, who offers him a job to find Gabriela and Laura for him, but he refuses him also. [[File:Logan(Film)Still31.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Logan confronts Donald Pierce.]] After some time Logan decides to reluctantly accept the job for Gabriela, only to find out that she has been murdered. Returning home and intent on getting Charles to safety, Logan is met by Donald Pierce once again, but knocked out by Laura, who snuck into his car on the way back. Logan gives Caliban the responsibility of dropping Pierce off on the side of the road as he gets Charles ready. However, Pierce returns with his cybernetic-enhanced enforcers, the Reavers, and Caliban is captured. Logan and Laura manage to escape them though. Later, Logan and Xavier learn through a video on Gabriela's phone that Transigen was breeding mutant children with DNA samples from several mutants for the "X-23" project, but found they were difficult to control as they grew up. Upon completion of the "X-24" project, the children were deemed unnecessary and were to be put to death. Gabriela helped several children escape from the Transigen compound before smuggling Laura across the border. Furthermore, Laura is revealed to be Logan's "daughter", as she was bred with his DNA from when he escaped from Alkali Lake in 1983. [[File:Logan(Film)Still40.jpg|thumb|250px|Logan kills a Transigen soldier while trying to rescue Laura and Xavier.]] The group decides to stay at a hotel in Oklahoma City. While there, Logan discovers that Eden doesn't exist, and is just a place referenced in one of the X-Men comic books that Laura brought with her. While Logan is out, the Reavers find their whereabouts, but Xavier suffers another seizure and freezes everyone in the hotel with a psychic blast, forcing Logan to painfully roam through the hotel. Making his way up, Logan kills the several Reavers surrounding Charles and injects Xavier with a suppressant before they leave the city, with the overall ordeal leaving hundreds injured and dozens dead. The trio is given shelter by a local family, the Munsons, after helping them round up their horses during a highway incident. When a pipe bursts on the family's property, Logan and Will Munson go to fix it where Logan drives off some [[Canewood Beverage|harassers]] of the Munsons. While they are out, [[X-24]], a feral clone of Wolverine without his own healing factor and a weaker adamantium skeleton, stabs Xavier through the chest, fatally wounding him, and slaughters Will's wife and son, and when they return, Will himself is mortally wounded. X-24 then captures Laura. Logan arrives and finds the injured Xavier, who dies shortly after. [[File:Logan(Film)Still37.jpg|left|thumb|250px|right|Logan battles X-24 at the farm.]] A vengeful Logan battles X-24 but is unable to overcome X-24's savagery as Caliban blows himself to let Laura and Logan escape by setting off two grenades which destroys the van he is in. Will then appears and impales X-24 on the forepart of a combine harvester with his car and then shoots X-24 in the head with a shotgun repeatedly before collapsing dead, allowing Logan and Laura to escape. After burying Xavier, an emotionally devastated Logan passes out after smashing the truck in far more rage, violence, and aggressiveness than before and wakes up in a clinic with the doctor attempting to convince him to get some treatment for his condition, but Logan refuses. Laura convinces Logan to take her to Eden and after a violent and aggressive argument, Logan gives in to show her it is not real. On the way there, Laura convinces Logan to sleep, and she drives them the rest of the way to Eden. [[File:Logan(Film)Still39.jpg|thumb|250px|Logan and Laura mourn Xavier.]] Logan and Laura arrive at Eden, a safe haven run by Rictor and the other Transigen test subjects who managed to escape. The children then put a weak and exhausted Logan to bed. Logan wakes up from the nightmares and Laura asks him to say that he had a nightmare and Logan ask her a question that she had nightmares and she's right that people hurt everybody. Laura had something in his shirt which it was a bullet, Logan says that it was made out of adamantium, the same kind which is put inside of them and it's probably what's killing them. Logan asks to hold this for him, which she does. The next morning, Laura and the children trim Logan's beard; when he wakes up, the children laugh at him. He walks up to the mirror and says that it's not funny. Logan then learns that the children plan to make a journey across the forest to the Canadian border and entrusts Laura to them. During their journey, the children are located and captured by the Reavers. Knowing the children need his help, Logan makes the fatal decision to use the serum left to him by Rictor to restore his healing factor which rapidly heals all his past scars and injuries but also has dangerous side effects once it wears off. He makes his way to the children and begins slaughtering the Reavers, but the serum quickly wears off as he meets [[Zander Rice]], the head of the Transigen project. Rice reveals that the destruction of mutantkind is due to a virus created by the Transigen Project, as an effort to control the mutant population and create their own for weaponization. He also mentions that Logan killed his father during his escape from the Weapon X program in Alkali Lake. Logan shoots Rice dead and Pierce releases X-24 on him, while the children wipe out the remaining Reavers before overwhelming Pierce and killing him. Logan and Laura proceed to fight X-24, with Rictor using his seismic powers to lift the earth under an armored truck and slam it on X-24. [[File:Logan(Film)Still33.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Logan battles Transigen soldiers in the forest.]] However, X-24 lifts the truck off of him and catches Logan off-guard, fatally impaling him on a tree stump, before Laura is able to kill X-24 with an adamantium bullet Logan had been holding on to if he ever wanted to kill himself. Now dying, Logan with his final breath tells Laura to not be the weapon Transigen wanted her to be, as Laura tearfully acknowledges Logan as her father, before Logan remarks at the feeling of death, before he dies in her arms. Laura and the children mourn his death, they bury him In Canada, his place of birth. Laura uses lines from the movie "Shane" on Logan's grave "A man has to be what he is, Joey. Can't break the mold. There's no living with the killing. There's no going back. Right or wrong, it's a brand. A Brand that sticks. Now you run on home to your mother... you tell her everything's alright. There are no more guns in the valley." And when she's done, Rictor tells the children that they gotta keep moving as they continue forward where they'll all be safe before Laura joins Rictor and the children she tearfully adjusts the cross on his grave, and places it on its side creating an "X" to honor her father as an X-Man and the Wolverine. His sacrifice saved the mutant race, meaning that mutants will be born again in the future. ====News from Deadpool==== [[File:X-Men Origins Wolverine (Deadpool 2 Post-Credits).png|thumb|250px|Wolverine speaking to Deadpool in the alternate timeline.]] The modern day Deadpool travelled back in time to the [[Three Mile Island Incident]], where he killed the alternate Deadpool that William Stryker created, telling Wolverine to return back to the fray should he ask despite his future retirement, leaving the confused Wolverine to agree.{{Cite film|Deadpool 2}} ====Legacy==== [[File:Logan's Skeleton with Deadpool.png|thumb|250px|left|Wolverine's skeleton is dug up by [[Deadpool]]]] Deadpool later learned from the Time Variance Authority that Wolverine was the "anchor being" of his timeline. With Wolverine dead, their timeline was dying. Deadpool attempted to bring back the original Wolverine by digging up his corpse, only to discover to his rage that it had been fully decomposed down to an adamantium skeleton. Deadpool used parts of Wolverine's corpse, including his claws, to fight off and massacre attacking agents from the Time Variance Authority. As a result of his world's Wolverine being permanently deceased, Deadpool sought out an [https://marvelcinematicuniverse.fandom.com/wiki/Wolverine alternate version] of Wolverine to replace the one who had died.{{Cite film|Deadpool & Wolverine}} ==Personality== {{Quote|Patience isn't my strongest suit.|Wolverine to [[Professor X]]|X-Men: Days of Future Past}} While he appears to be perpetually angry, he is a rather complicated man, due to his violent and mysterious past. He has very little patience for those around him, and this quality adds to his gruff demeanor and solo attitude. Indeed, Logan prefers to be alone due to him disliking and distrusting the company of others. Despite his disgruntled nature, he has a very dry sense of humor, constantly insulting others and giving them demeaning nicknames. Logan does not hesitate to harm or kill and seems not to harbor any true remorse or guilt over any violent actions he has done in the past, with the exception of Jean Grey in the erased timeline.{{Cite film|X-Men: Days of Future Past}} His past has, in fact, emotionally scarred and forced him to make himself numb to the pain and suffering he has endured, from his childhood trauma to his several lifetimes as a soldier in many of the most violent wars in modern history. His is therefore less inclined to show mercy towards his opponents, anyone else that attacks him, or those who he thinks deserve it, such as when he tried to kill [[Noburo Mori]] since he was corrupt and took part in Mariko's kidnapping to be paid a fortune.{{Cite film|The Wolverine}} Despite his willingness to use violence, he is still a moral person who does not do so needlessly, nor does he attack innocent people. While he is described as cynical and pessimistic, Logan is nonetheless a good person and will always stand up for those who can't defend themselves, or at the very least be willing to avenge them, as such as his killing of [[Agent Zero]] for attacking him and killing the elderly couple that sheltered him. While hiding in a well from the Nagasaki nuclear bombing, he was not fazed by the destruction going on outside since he could do nothing about it, but was still willing to save [[Ichirō Yashida]] when he had the chance to, unable to leave the man to die. However, when Yashida proved himself to be evil and repeatedly tried killing Logan to gain his healing as Silver Samurai, Logan tore the suit open, pulling the wires that kept Yashida alive, before stabbing his chest and dropped him off a cliff to his death in a rage. Logan has grown some restraint and perhaps mercy over the years presumably because of his prolonged exposure to the X-Men. Although he has no restraint or regret for unleashing his animal side on those deserving of such violence, he does not show cruelty to those who do not deserve it, for example, he shows mercy to the the Pentagon guards by non-lethally incapacitating them while attempting to free Magneto with Charles and Beast. Although officially an X-Man, Logan prefers to be alone. He has a deep distrust and an inherent lack of respect for people, regardless of whether they are both human and mutant, having seen nearly 200 years of violence, prejudice, and war.{{Cite film|X-Men}} This attitude also stems from having people he trusted betray him, such as [[Kayla Silverfox|Kayla]], and his brother, Victor. However, the true reason for Logan's wanting to be alone is due to the fact that so many people tend to die around him, such as friends and loved ones. He also shows signs of self loathing for the violent life he has lived and the pain he has endured. Logan is extremely loyal to and protective over the few who can gain his trust and respect. He is completely ready to sacrifice his life if it will save someone he loves, and will stop at nothing to protect his friends and family. Despite his blunt personality, Logan has made many friends with the X-Men, but most notably Rogue, becoming almost a father figure to her, after realizing that both of them are outcasts. His is also one of Xavier's most trusted allies, with Xavier even having a mentor role to Logan. He grew attached to Charles to the point of breaking down when he was disintegrated by Dark Phoenix.{{Cite film|X-Men: The Last Stand}} He is also greatly attached to [[Mariko Yashida]], having found a chance of happiness with her, to the point of risking his life to defend her while his healing factor was disabled, fight an army of [[Black Clan]] ninjas, and even torture a member of the Yakuza to find her. Logan is a man of vice; he loves to drink alcohol and smoke cigars, knowing that neither can effect his health due to his powers.{{Cite film|X-Men: First Class}} He is also an adamant motorcycle rider and has shown to have a hatred/fear of flying in planes. His most famous traits are his temper and attitude. Logan seems constantly angry and has a very short temper. Though he is capable of deep thought, he has said himself that he has an animalistic side to his personality. This side of him is feral and primal, causing him to become instinctual and go berserk, especially in combat. It is caused by his animalistic mutation and triggered by rage, fear, frustration, great annoyance, general distress, or pain. It results in him becoming highly aggressive and mindlessly brutal, similar to an animal, as he is prone to roar with rage in most of these cases. In some cases, it is this brute-like side of Logan that make it easy for him kill and harm without hesitation.{{Cite film|X2}}{{Cite film|X-Men: Apocalypse}} After the events of [[Logan (film)|2029]], having long since lost all of his loved ones (excluding Charles), combined with his gradually weakening body, Logan's outlook on life has become bleak at best. More hostile than ever, Logan cares little for helping others and seeks only to find a small measure of personal peace by becoming a drifter on the sea. With his healing powers so weak that he is no longer immune to toxins, he has taken solace in drinking. However, upon learning of his estranged biological daughter/clone, Laura, and a series of events that would help her, Wolverine ultimately regains his compassion and hope from years ago. ==Powers and Abilities== {{Quote|What you have is a gift.|[[Kayla Silverfox]]|X-Men Origins: Wolverine}} ===Powers=== *'''Superhuman Strength''': His body is naturally evolved and conditioned to the peak of human physical potential and continually adjusting to various forms of damage. After his skeleton was laced with the indestructible metal alloy adamantium, he could still effortlessly carry himself. He can overpower large full grown humans with ease, able to hurl them with a single hand and throw them up to 50 feet, as well as easily defeat and overpower up to a dozen grown men in hand-to-hand combat, and he has the ability to knock out any man completely unconscious with one punch to the face. His monstrous physical strength also extends to his legs, as he is able to jump several feet into the air. Even in his weakened state in 2029, he was strong enough to break a man's rifle in half over his knee. *'''Superhuman Durability''': Logan's body and tissue is far more durable than a normal human's. His durability was further enhanced after his bones were infused with adamantium; to the point that it is extremely difficult, if not impossible, to kill or injure him. He has been repeatedly shot, stabbed, and has endured extreme blunt force trauma without passing out and being slowed down. In some instances, bullets have ricocheted off of his adamantium skeleton, barely piercing the skin. In fact, his body is durable to the point where he can survive the effects of a nuclear explosion, as well as the disintegrating effects of the all-powerful and unimaginably destructive Phoenix force with only a few layers of skin being destroyed, making Logan virtually indestructible, next to invincible. *'''Superhuman Speed''': Logan is able to run over 60 mph and move with reflexes unobtainable by normal human beings. When sensing Victor was near and going after Kayla, he was able to leave his job site and reach her car in minutes. Despite the effects of adamantium poisoning weakening him and causing him to age, Logan was able to catch up to Laura and the group of mutants even though they had already trekked a great distance. After injecting himself with the Transigen serum, he was able to compete with a moving vehicle. [[File:Logan_Berserk_In_Forest.jpg|thumb|250px|Wolverine leaping at a [[Reavers|Reaver]] in the forest]] *'''Superhuman Agility''': Logan has remarkable equilibrium and coordination, greater than any human athlete. He is capable of scaling vertical surfaces with remarkable efficiency. He is graceful and has a large range of motion, able to pursue and outrun a target in various types of terrains. His skills allow him to fluidly dodge and attack from different directions without losing balance. He has shown noticeable acrobatics, even lunging from trees to attack his target. {{Quote|I ignored my instincts. And I ignored what I really am. And that won't ever happen again.|Wolverine|X-Men Origins: Wolverine}} *'''Superhuman Senses''': Logan's senses are heightened to levels similar to various animals. He can track down and identify people through their scent alone, as well as detect their presence within a large area through scent and sound. This makes one disguising themselves, even [[Mystique]] using her shape-shifting powers, pointless, due to the inability to change their scent. His hearing is equally acute, able to clearly hear the low whisper of [[William Stryker]] while submerged in water. He can see much further and closer than normal humans with perfect clarity, even in total darkness. His senses enable him to sense when danger is near, such as at [[Ichirō Yashida|Yashida]]'s funeral. However, he began to require corrective lenses after his adamantium skeleton gradually poisoned him. **'''Animal Empathy''': Logan can sense the emotional state of animals on a basic level such as fear, rage, happiness or pain. He can communicate with them to make them aware of his actions and intent. This allows for dangerous animals to leave him alone rather than attacking him as would they normally would attack other people. {{Quote|He has uncharted regenerative capabilities, enabling him to heal rapidly.|[[Jean Grey]]|X-Men (film)}} [[File:Put_back_together_as_he_is_torn_apart.jpeg|thumb|250px|Wolverine's healing factor against the Phoenix]] *'''Regenerative Healing Factor''': Logan can heal and regenerate all physical and mental harm to himself almost instantly. He has an extremely powerful regenerative healing factor, being able to fully heal, regenerate and recover from virtually anything and everything almost instantly, such as powerful acids, poison, fire, severe burns, crush or broken bones, stab wounds, impaling, mass explosions, nuclear explosions, bombs, being shot with hand guns, shotguns, assault rifles, .50 caliber machine guns, and miniguns, extreme physical force, and even from the nigh-omnipotent and unimaginably destructive Pheonix Force. Any damaged area or lost anatomy will be completely restored with no lingering side effects in seconds, even forcing objects embedded out to heal properly. Logan can rapidly regenerate from virtually anything and everything, such as gunshots to the head or body, stab wounds, bombs, fire explosions, poison, and nuclear bombs, among many other things. Even if he dies in a medical sense, he can quickly resuscitate himself, making him virtually immortal. Despite being shot twice in the head and losing his memories, Logan was able to regenerate his brain cells and eventually regain his lost memories years later.
